programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Part one of two. Medical student Alex asks Mark to help clear an old friend mixed up in the murder of a Los Angeles madam, while Madison Wesley is stalked by a killer who has recently undergone plastic surgery. Dick Van Dyke and Joanna Cassidy star

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Part two of two. Madison moves in with her new love, unaware of his true identity and murderous intentions. Meanwhile, Jesse falls under suspicion of writing a scandalous novel revealing spicy details about hospital workers. Starring Joanna Cassidy and Dick Van Dyke

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: Tina, a woman with romance on her mind, gets Kimble a job at a small animal hospital. However, things turn sour when Kimble rejects her advances

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.desc: When the co-owner of a club is charged with murdering his business partner, the key witness is a woman who was running a blackmail scam on him

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: Part two of two. Walker's river rafting holiday becomes dangerous when one of the group is murdered

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: A murder inquiry puts Mac and Stella on the trail of a dangerous gang that kills for sport. Meanwhile, Danny and Aiden investigate the death of a woman who worked at an illegal massage parlour. Crime drama, starring Gary Sinise, Melina Kanakaredes and Carmine Giovinazzo

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The notorious Tri-State Sniper appears to have claimed another victim when a petty officer is shot dead in a crowded park. However, when the team discovers the killer in question was already in custody on the day of the shooting, it becomes clear a copy-cat is on the loose. As their investigation progresses, the agents learn the victim was recently found guilty of placing a camera in the female changing rooms of the USS Camden, earning himself plenty of enemies - but did any of them hate him enough to kill?

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: The team uncovers an alliance between al-Qaeda and a Mexican drug cartel when it investigates a shoot-out at a pawn shop. As the agents explore the reasons behind the unusual coalition, a Mexican police chief with a vendetta against the Mendoza gang joins them

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: When tragedy strikes, Richard Kimble risks a visit to his sister Donna in his old hometown though he is certain Gerard won't be far

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.desc: Ex-con Tony Polk delivers gifts dressed as a pirate, but when a recipient is murdered with a hook and a witness claims Tony did it, Perry steps in to help

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Abby babysits a bright nine-year-old while the other members of the team try to locate the youngster's missing father - a survivalist wanted in connection with a case of robbery and murder. The search takes them deep into the wilderness - where a startling discovery awaits. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Sean Murray

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: A karate student joins a gang to avenge those responsible for shooting his brother, and Walker attempts to restore order to the neighbourhood before Christmas

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team members are surprised when Gibbs asks DiNozzo's father to join them in their investigation of a murder committed at the US Naval Academy. However, Tony Sr's experience turns out to play a vital role in the inquiry, which finds the team members hunting for a missing nuclear weapon they suspect is about to be sold on the black market. Guest starring Robert Wagner

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Hetty enlists the help of a former colleague when an ex-Navy intelligence officer is murdered, but things go awry when her old friend threatens to kill her. The team races to save Hetty and expose her one-time workmate as having lied to the victim in order to make money from a seemingly charitable venture

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A traumatised marine escapes from a psychiatric unit, convinced he is still fighting in Iraq. The agents believe his erratic behaviour indicates post-traumatic stress disorder and try to calmly talk him round before restraining him. However, blood tests reveal high levels of steroids in his system - meaning someone is using him as a human lab rat. Starring Lauren Holly, Sean Murray and Cote de Pablo

programme.desc: A Muslim US Marine is found seemingly shot dead near a mosque that has been linked to an al-Qaeda recruiter. programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Havers returns to work and is forced to confront demons from her past as the team investigates the case of a woman murdered with a shotgun. However, the team refuses to give up, and finds evidence of a cover-up involving top officials and a multi-national drugs company. Guest starring Meera Syal and Lorraine Ashbourne

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: A severed finger is brought to the Lyell Centre by DCI Rory Goodchild and immigration enforcement officer Pia Butler, who ask Nikki and Thomas to test for a DNA match with 17 year-old Syrian illegal immigrant Akka Khoury. The teenager was sent the digit by a people-smuggler who claims to have her mother and is demanding money for her safe return. Meanwhile, Akka's 12 year-old cousin Sosa is trying to enter the UK illegally hidden in a van on a cross-channel ferry. Emilia Fox stars

programme.title: Cadfael
programme.desc: The annual St Peter's Fair gets under way in Shrewsbury but a gruesome murder dampens the festivities. Antagonism between local merchants and visiting traders becomes increasingly ugly, and as civil unrest rages in the streets, Beringar is ordered to kill his friend Cadfael, whose investigations are causing King Stephen acute political embarrassment. Medieval whodunit, starring Derek Jacobi, Eoin McCarthy and Michael Culver

programme.desc: The judge hears the case of three men accused of shooting a rival gang member - but the trial is in danger of collapsing when the prosecution's main witness is killed in a hit-and-run incident. programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Pilot episode of the crime drama, starring Amanda Redman as Superintendent Sandra Pullman, a rising star of the Met who is sidelined after a failed hostage rescue. Put in charge of a new department, she is dismayed to discover the unit is to be manned by a motley crew of old-school former detectives who know little about modern police procedure. However, when they re-examine the case of a man whose conviction for murder 20 years ago has just been overturned, their combined experience and unorthodox methods bring interesting results. Alun Armstrong, Dennis Waterman and James Bolam play Redman's veteran team, with Jon Finch, Tim Woodwork and Philip Bretherton

programme.desc: Feature-length episode. programme.desc: Tony tries to help Jacko forget Julie by taking him on a double date, while Jessica exposes corruption in high places. The McCleods hear the patter of tiny feet and recent events finally take their toll on Mark, who makes an inspection of the barracks go with something of a bang

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: James makes an eye-opening call to Lord Hulton to deal with a runaway horse, and his failure to save a dog brings shocking consequences. Christopher Timothy and Robert Hardy star

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: Tinker lectures Lovejoy on the importance of not abusing his ability to gather ill-gotten gains. However, the antiques dealer briefly turns his back on his own ethical standards, only to soon regret his transgression. Ian McShane and Dudley Sutton star

programme.title: Fresh Fields
programme.desc: Emma is forced to rearrange her plans for the festive period at the last minute, leaving Hester and William disappointed they will not get to spend their first Christmas with their grandson. However, it remains to be seen who will help them consume all the extra food and drink they have to hand. Christmas special of the comedy, starring Anton Rodgers and Julia McKenzie

programme.title: Waiting for God
programme.desc: Diana tries her hand at being nice for a 'glamorous granny' competition at Bayview. Stephanie Cole stars

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Lieutenant Commander Willoughby takes an instant liking to Compo, Clegg and Foggy and offers them the chance to become extremely wealthy. Ron Moody guest stars

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Feeling rejected by Nora Batty, Compo reminisces about the first love of his life in his primary schooldays. With help from Norman and Foggy, he tries to find her while there is still time. Bill Owen stars

programme.title: Dalziel & Pascoe
programme.desc: A landlady's husband is driven insane by jealousy, taking the regulars at their pub hostage as he attempts to find out whether she has been unfaithful. Little does he realise that among his captives is an off-duty Dalziel - who must try to avert disaster as the siege spirals out of control. Feature-length detective drama, starring Warren Clarke and Colin Buchanan, with Dennis Waterman

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Pilot episode of the crime drama, starring Amanda Redman as Superintendent Sandra Pullman, a rising star of the Met who is sidelined after a failed hostage rescue. Put in charge of a new department, she is dismayed to discover the unit is to be manned by a motley crew of old-school former detectives who know little about modern police procedure. However, when they re-examine the case of a man whose conviction for murder 20 years ago has just been overturned, their combined experience and unorthodox methods bring interesting results. Alun Armstrong, Dennis Waterman and James Bolam play Redman's veteran team, with Jon Finch, Tim Woodwork and Philip Bretherton

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. The body of 20 year-old Lorna Katz is discovered drifting in the River Lea, with evidence suggesting she was tied up somewhere before she floated downstream. However, the death takes on an ever-more sinister significance when the team discovers that Lorna was the best friend of Ruth Tresize, a teenager who disappeared three years previously, with the missing girl's mother Ali becoming fixated on Nikki for information, convinced that the two incidents are connected

programme.title: Cadfael
programme.desc: Feature-length drama in which the Middle Ages' answer to Sherlock Holmes sets out to investigate a slave girl's disappearance and locate a treasured relic missing from his abbey. Unfortunately, no sooner has he begun searching for a connection than a dead body turns up on his doorstep, plunging him into a mire of murder and subterfuge. Starring Derek Jacobi, Terrence Hardiman, Anthony Green, George Irving and Louise Delamere

programme.desc: Dorien takes her antique silver samovar to the Antiques Roadshow, inspiring the sisters to search for their own family heirlooms - and soon they make a surprising discovery

programme.title: Rownd a Rownd
programme.desc: Dani is having a little difficulty trying to sort out her new estate and Barry comes to her rescue and it seems that both have forgotten any past disagreements. Caitlin and Sian are becoming friendlier and the youngster finally agrees to meet up with the appropriate authorities to ask for help. Mathew's dependence on painkillers pushes him to ask Vince for some assistance

programme.title: Gwesty Parc y Stradey
programme.desc: It's a big day for seven sisters from Penygroes, Gwynedd, as they celebrate Gwyneth's 70th birthday. On a bright summer's day they reminisce over scones and clotted cream. Meanwhile, after falling off his bike and breaking his leg, chef Stuart heads to his mother's house for her home cooking and takes inspiration from her orchard. Documentary offering an insight into the inner workings of four-star Stradey Park Hotel in Llanelli, Carmarthenshire

Meanwhile, a semi in Olton in the Midlands and a terrace in South Norwood in London are also sold under the hammer. Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Lucy Alexander meet the new owners to hear plans for their buys

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: Questions are asked when a year after a house fire a second claim comes in, while the data embedded in digital photographs submitted to support stolen items does not add up

programme.desc: In Glasgow, a call about a possible person in the Clyde triggers a huge emergency response, while in Edinburgh, the Special Operations team are called to a fire in a block of flats

programme.desc: Sonali Shah helps a couple find a new home in Somerset where they can create their very own back garden vegetable patch. Away from the search, Sonali also learns about the potentially perilous Bristol Channel and meets the volunteers who operate two life-saving rescue hovercrafts

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore rescues faded curtains, a 1950s chest of drawers and an old storage larder from the tip, enlisting bag-maker Rosanna Gethin and screen-print artist Zoe Murphy to help transform them

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The experts use their restoration skills on an ancient penny farthing bicycle, a battered leather briefcase and a clocking-on clock from the British Rail depot in Crewe

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Michael Ball present the live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: A frustrated Nancy tries to track down a lost package, leaving her family curious over what could be so important - especially when she refuses to look after Ollie. Isaac has questions about his family, and Bernie is surprised when Molly apologises and asks her out for a coffee

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Louis feels trapped as Vicky's blackmailing escalates, and he wonders how much longer he can keep his double life a secret. Max grows more determined to catch the hospital thief, a desperate Dom campaigns for Ange to assist with his operation and Kian leads Lucky on a merry dance across the hospital as he tries to dodge a drugs test

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: Patients from Britain's most remote community - on Fair Isle, south of Shetland - wait for a weather window to make the trip to Lerwick for important operations

programme.title: The Syndicate
programme.desc: The friends desperately rush Roxy to hospital but are horrified to discover that emergency medical treatment is hugely expensive. Unless they can get the money to pay it off, Roxy won't be allowed to leave the country. In all the confusion, Keeley realises they have missed out on their kidnap plan. Time is running out for the syndicate - and they are still no closer to getting proof. Unknown to them, however, Frank is confronted by Mr Shriver, the head of Mercury Millions' fraud team. Does he know about the stolen ticket?

programme.title: Glow Up: Britain's Next Make-Up Star
programme.desc: The contest for aspiring make-up artists returns, with new host Maya Jama. The contestants' first professional assignment sees them creating an eye-catching look for a Superdrug beauty campaign. In the creative brief, judges Val Garland and Dominic Skinner ask the artists to show what makes them unique. Only then do the judges decide which two will take on the first elimination face-off

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Norman questions Norma's faith in him, and Romero's presented with evidence that may change the course of Miss Watson's murder investigation. Meanwhile, Zane's thirst for revenge threatens Dylan. A prequel to Alfred Hitchcock's Psycho, based on Robert Bloch's novel of the same name, depicting the life of Norman Bates and his mother Norma prior to the events portrayed in the film

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Norma refuses to tell Norman the truth about his blackouts, while Romero digs deeper into the death of Miss Watson. A prequel to Alfred Hitchcock's Psycho, based on Robert Bloch's novel of the same name, depicting the life of Norman Bates and his mother Norma prior to the events portrayed in the film

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: An amateur detective turns the tables on fraudsters running a crash-for-cash scam, and fur flies as a woman claiming her pedigree cat has been stolen is caught out by social media

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: Paramedics respond to a major crash on the M8. In Edinburgh, specialist paramedic Mat is called to a head injury before being diverted to a more serious life-or-death incident

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning hosts from Southwell Racecourse in Nottinghamshire, where experts Thomas Forrester and Richard Madley help competing teams bag the best buys to make a profit at auction in neighbouring Lincolnshire

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: The medics and their staff get to work on the day of the charity car wash, where Luca, Ruhma and Karen have to go the extra mile for their customers

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley helps a couple living in London's Docklands to find a home with green space in Buckinghamshire's Chiltern Hills. She also pays a visit to the Henley Regatta

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Designer Jacqui Joseph swoops in on an old ottoman, some rusty garden gates and unwanted printer's trays, enlisting upholsterer Sharon O'Connor and blacksmith Kev Paxton to turn them into profit-makers

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Dom Chinea tackles the challenge of a battered old barber's pole and Lucia Scalisi takes on the restoration of a significant early work by eminent Nigerian artist Ben Enwonwu

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Party Election Broadcast
programme.desc: By a candidate for the London mayoral election.

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Michael Ball present the live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Arit Anderson and the Rich brothers are in Luton, where they meet Elizabeth and Benjamin Lovett. They and their children are a fun-loving family but their garden is a drab, which is a shame as they practically live in it. The outdoor space is very special to them as they have numerous special needs which make going out very challenging. Whose design will the Lovetts love most?

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: A Gloucestershire woman brings in her King Charles II painting, hoping Lucia Scalisi can restore it to its crowning glory, and silversmith Brenton West works on a well-travelled trunk, which holds cherished memories for a mother and daughter. Goldsmith Richard Talman tries to reunite the broken pieces of an engagement ring and toy restorers Julie and Amanda revive a toy donkey that comforted a young child after he lost his mother and his sister

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: It's summer week in the sewing room as the contestants are challenged to create three garments too look and feel good in the heat. They begin by following the pattern for a pair of paperbag shorts, before transforming second-hand men's swimming gear into a women's cocktail outfit. The made-to-measure challenge sees them taking on button-down sundresses, and eagle-eyed judges Patrick Grant and Esme Young will be looking for perfectly aligned buttonholes and secure fastenings. Who will miss the mark and be second to leave? Joe Lycett has the answer

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Highlights of the latest Premier League fixtures, featuring Tottenham Hotspur v Southampton at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium and Aston Villa v Manchester City at Villa Park. Spurs were looking to boost their European football aspirations by emulating their impressive 5-2 victory in the reverse fixture, while City looked to move a step closer to the title. Plus, a look back at last night's contest between Chelsea and Brighton & Hove Albion

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, and a look at the biggest issues in the women's game. Arsenal are the defending champions, but face stiff competition from the likes of Chelsea, Manchester City and Manchester United

programme.title: I Can See Your Voice
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness hosts the mystery singing game show, in which contestants aided by a celebrity panel must spot good singers without hearing them. Regular celebrity investigators Jimmy Carr, Alison Hammond and Amanda Holden are joined by guest Nadine Coyle, as they assist two contestants from London in picking out the best singers and winning up to £10,000

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The team visits a couple who bought a prefabricated bungalow in Chesterton, Staffordshire, as well as first-time developers in Sutton in Surrey and Bilston in the West Midlands. Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell present

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: A fraudster feigns innocence when caught scamming a holiday company. Elsewhere, CCTV shows that an employee claiming compensation was actually drunk when he fell

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: In Glasgow, ambulance crew Gary and Stephen are called to help a patient with a rare autoimmune disease who has lost mobility. In Edinburgh, Moira and Blair treat a young man with severe abdominal pain. And there is a serious car crash just outside the city. In Aberdeen, paramedics Ewan and Alan give emergency treatment to a lady whose heartbeat is dangerously fast and rush her to hospital

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles presents from Newbury, Berkshire, with experts Caroline Hawley and David Harper ready to help the blue and red teams maximise their profit at auction. The presenter also recalls the world's largest peace camp

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Karen intervenes when a bitterly divorced couple arrive for appointments at the same time. Medical drama series set in a busy Midlands surgery, with Jan Pearson

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson is in Derbyshire with a recently married couple making an epic location shift, swapping the Blue Mountains in Australia for the wilds of the Peak District. They have a significant budget of £750,000 and have their hearts set on a character home with a large garden for their three dogs. Jules also encounters an unlikely centre of manufacturing nestled in the county's rural countryside

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore enlists the help of expert furniture artist Chloe Kempster to set to work on a reclaimed chest of drawers, while Guy Trench needs to put his best foot forward with some dated shoe lasts. Sarah sets herself the challenge of repurposing some rusty hanging baskets

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Upholsterer Sonnaz Nooranvary revamps a family treasure with an intriguing history - a threadbare theatre chair that once played host to thousands of theatre-goers and bingo players in the auditorium of the Derby Hippodrome. A rare timepiece that has not worked for years and a cherished bronze locket also receive attention

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Party Election Broadcast
programme.desc: A party election broadcast for the London mayoral election

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones hosts the live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Patrick opens up to Isaac about his brother Paul, and how he wishes he could have opened up about the trouble he was in - the trouble that eventually got him killed. Billy and Honey discover that Amy has been posting nasty things about Janet on social media and Bernie overhears Rainie and Stuart talking about finding a surrogate again

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Industry titans Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden, Touker Suleyman, Tej Lalvani and Sara Davies are entertained by a DJ and dancer pitching a socially distanced nightclub business, while an Irish inventor with a muscle massaging device hopes his proposition will loosen up the Dragons' pockets. A pair of optical consultants have their sights set on investment with their ready-readers business and a 29-year-old tech enthusiast is confident that his clever way of monetising people's personal online data. Presented by Evan Davis

programme.title: This Is My House
programme.desc: Joe Sugg and Richard Madeley join regular panellists Bill Bailey, Emily Atack and Judi Love in trying to guess which of the four Clares is the real owner of a property in Guildford, Surrey. Those trying to pass themselves off as the lady of the manor include a former air hostess and a 'Klara' from Slovakia, who says most people think she is the cleaner not the homeowner. Presented by Stacey Dooley

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate, inviting a panel of politicians and other guests to answer questions from a virtual audience on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Adam Fleming, Laura Kuenssberg and Chris Mason discuss the week's biggest stories as they round-up recent events in Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Greta Thunberg: A Year to Change the World
programme.desc: Greta sets off to the annual World Economic Forum in Davos, Switzerland, to call for an immediate reduction in fossil fuel use, but is frustrated by what she sees as a media obsession constantly pitting her against Donald Trump. As she travels across Europe, Greta meets Polish miners who fear that climate change policies will further decimate their industry and culture, and discovers the prototype of a technology that aims to capture carbon dioxide from factory emissions before it enters the atmosphere. She also meets one of her inspirations - Sir David Attenborough

programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don adds self-sown ornamental grass seedlings to the paradise garden at Longmeadow, and Advolly Richmond explores the extraordinary history of lawns. Plus, Adam Frost explores the front gardens in a London street

programme.title: The Super League Show
programme.desc: Tanya Arnold introduces action from the latest fixtures, including St Helens v Wakefield Trinity, Hull FC v Warrington Wolves and Castleford Tigers v Leigh Centurions

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the morning session on day four at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, including the conclusion of Jack Lisowski's first-round match against Ali Carter and the opening frames of Mark Allen v Lyu Haotian

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the afternoon session on day four at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, where Ding Junhui's first-round match against Stuart Bingham concludes and 2019 champion Judd Trump plays the opening frames of his contest against Liam Highfield

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman, Vick Hope, Gethin Jones and Tim Vine go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Saved and Remade
programme.desc: The transformation of a beloved car leaves its owner astounded, and a pair of ballet shoes are given a new lease of life. Hosted by Sabrina Grant

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Mediterranean Adventure
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore the culinary delights of Sardinia, starting out in Cagliari, where everyone is in high spirits for the feast of St Efisio. The bikers join in by cooking a local speciality, almond and orange cakes. They also meet the tuna fishermen of Carloforte and a chef who has a special way of serving up platters of the fish, are shown how to make sheep's black pudding by a group of shepherds and are served a stew by the 'King of Sardinia'

programme.title: All That Glitters: Britain's Next Jewellery Star
programme.desc: Taking place in a specially built workshop nestled in Birmingham's historic Jewellery Quarter, Katherine Ryan hosts as talented jewellers battle it out to prove they can be the next big jewellery-making sensation. This time, the seven remaining contestants are challenged to make a chain collar and a sweetheart brooch, before one of them leaves the competition

programme.title: Makeup: A Glamorous History
programme.desc: Makeup artist Lisa Eldridge explores what the beauty looks of three iconic moments in British history reveal about that era, beginning with the high Georgians. Lisa goes to Chatsworth House in Derbyshire in search of one of the beauty icons of the day, Georgiana, Duchess of Devonshire, and discovers how in this period of extreme wealth in Britain, the rich entered an arms race of beauty. Later, she recreates an authentic Georgian look on a 21st century model

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Ardal O'Hanlon plays host in this edition of the comedy showcase, recorded before a packed house at London's Hammersmith Apollo. The show features sets by Mawaan Rizwan and Sara Barron

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Emily Maitlis

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Action from the evening session on day four at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, including the concluding session of Mark Allen's first-round match against Lyu Hoatian

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship Extra
programme.desc: A look back at the first-round matches on the fourth day of the tournament at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield

programme.title: Louis Theroux: Shooting Joe Exotic
programme.desc: Louis revisits his 2011 documentary America's Most Dangerous Pets, returning to Oklahoma to explore what has happened since. Joe Exotic, aka Joseph Allen Maldonado-Passage, now resides in a federal prison after being found guilty of a murder-for-hire plot and multiple animal cruelty charges, and having been the subject of hit Netflix documentary Tiger King

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff, Chris Harris and Paddy McGuinness confront the male mid-life crisis by buying the perfect sports cars for a middle-aged gentleman. Back at the track, Chris tests out the raucous new Toyota GR Yaris hot hatch

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: John Torode and Gregg Wallace present the first episode of finals week, in which the five remaining contenders must cook a five-course lunch at the City of London's Plaisterers Hall for members of the hospitality sector who delivered thousands of meals to frontline workers during the first lockdown. The contestants must each serve up as dish of their own creation, inspired by their own lockdown experience, then return to the studio to make culinary tributes to their heroes

programme.desc: A pair of university graduates hope that their organic superfood powders will supercharge the Dragons' pockets. A low-calorie craft lager business brews up trouble over its high valuation, a dental-minded duo demonstrate their plaque-detecting app, while there's a heartfelt pitch from an entrepreneur hoping to up-scale her range of afro and curly hair products. Whether Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden, Tej Lalvani, Sara Davies or Touker Suleyman will be prompted to invest in all - or any - of the products being pitched remains to be seen. Presented by Evan Davis

programme.title: Saved and Remade
programme.desc: A collection of beautiful Middle Eastern fabrics and some vintage game consoles are among today's transformations. Hosted by Sabrina Grant

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Mediterranean Adventure
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers discover the culinary delights of Corsica, starting out in a delicatessen to get a taste of the local cuisine, including the figatelli sausage. The owner explains how most of the island's food is made up of a holy trinity of ingredients - meat, cheese and chestnuts, a list that forms the basis of the bikers' exploration

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Four chefs, combining a mix of exciting new talent and returning contestants, compete for the North East, Yorkshire and Humber region with canapes, starters and fish dishes inspired by British invention and innovation. After their unique creations have been judged, one of them must leave the competition

programme.title: Bent Coppers: Crossing the Line of Duty
programme.desc: Exploring the sleazy underbelly of London's Soho in the early 1970s, where an explosion of dirty pornographic bookshops provides an open invitation for corrupt police to be greedy. With insider interviewers and archive footage, this episode continues to unearth the lawless underworld of 1970s London, audacious levels of police corruption and the birth of the internal force tasked with combating it

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Guests Phill Jupitus, Holly Walsh and Nish Kumar join regular panellist Alan Davies and host Sandi Toksvig to contemplate the theme 'nonsense' in an extended episode. As usual, correctness and even intelligence go out of the window in this quiz, in which the questions are so difficult points are awarded for the answers its questionmaster finds most interesting

programme.title: The Detectives: Fighting Organised Crime
programme.desc: When a man is gunned down on a busy residential street in Manchester, a team of specialist GMP detectives race to identify the masked men responsible

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: The final four amateur cooks interpret a classic dish of their choice before the best three face a Chef's Table challenge at Michel Roux Jr's acclaimed restaurant Le Gavroche, As usual, John Torode and Gregg Wallace assess the participants' skills to a bid to assess that they can be worthy finalists, joined in their endeavours by Monica Galetti, Steve Groves and Michel's daughter Emily Roux

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: The team returns to Beechgrove's home turf and after a year away from the garden. Carole and Brian set up neighbouring patches for some healthy competition, while Head Gardener Mairi reclaims Beechgrove after its time on furlough in 2020. George is back chopping down trees in his home garden in Joppa, while Kirsty builds a stylish greenhouse on her brand-new ornamental allotment

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Designer Jacqui Joseph swoops in on an old ottoman, some rusty garden gates and unwanted printer's trays, enlisting upholsterer Sharon O'Connor and blacksmith Kev Paxton to turn them into profit-makers

programme.title: Chris Packham's Animal Einsteins
programme.desc: Chris takes a look at nature's best navigators, with mule deer building up complicated memory maps of their landscapes so they don't get lost, while elephants rely on the knowledge of the herd's matriarch to find precious waterholes. Dung beetles plot routes by reading the stars, while the mantis shrimp has one of the most complex navigation systems in the natural world. For many species of fish and birds, there's safety in numbers and they often travel in vast shoals and flocks - but who leads the way, and how do they decide?

programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: The afternoon session on day six at the Crucible, featuring the conclusion of Shaun Murphy v Mark Davis in round one and the opening frames of Ronnie O'Sullivan v Anthony McGill or Ricky Walden in round two

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman, Vick Hope, Gethin Jones and Tim Vine test their skills in a variety of trivia-based games, with the ultimate winner declared on Friday

programme.title: Saved and Remade
programme.desc: A vintage sewing table and a martial arts expert's karate belts are among the transformations tackled by the team of skilled craftspeople. Sabrina Grant hosts

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Mediterranean Adventure
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore the culinary delights of Provence in southern France. Starting out in Marseille, they meet a Moroccan woman who runs a welcome centre where immigrants can eat together - and who makes a fish stew to die for. Travelling west, they learn about the culinary influence of the Romans who conquered Europe 2,000 years ago, try a breakfast of pastry filled with spicy octopus, and dine with a Catalan farmer and his family on a regional speciality of barbecued snails

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: For their main courses, one chef is showcasing fallow deer in their dish celebrating the invention of cats eyes. Another pays homage to the Humber Bridge, and the final chef pays tribute to a miners' lamp with a dish centred on mutton. Whose inventiveness in the kitchen will veteran judge Simon Rogan rate the highest? Andi Oliver presents

programme.title: Amazing Hotels: Life Beyond the Lobby
programme.desc: Giles Coren and Monica Galetti join the staff working in some of the most unusual hotels around the world, learning the tricks of the trade

programme.title: Don Rodolfo
programme.desc: Facing certain death, Don Rodolfo, the world's greatest swordsman, lover and butthead, tells his epic tale. Comedy short based on the hit live show of the same name, starring Ciaran Dowd and Jayde Adams

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara O Briain hosts as regular panellist Hugh Dennis looks back on the latest events in news and politics, with the help of Maisie Adam, Ed Byrne, Ed Gamble, Sukh Ojla and Mark Simmons. Yet again, stand-up and improvisation are the name of the game as the contestants try to get one up on one another

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Kirsty Wark

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: The evening session on day six at the Crucible, featuring the concluding session of Mark Selby's first-round match against Kurt Maflin and the start of Neil Robertson v Jack Lisowski or Ali Carter in round two

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship Extra
programme.desc: A look back at the first and second-round matches on the sixth day of the tournament at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield

programme.title: Football's Darkest Secret
programme.desc: The documentary moves to Newcastle with the stories of survivors who suffered at the hands of George Ormond, a youth coach connected to Newcastle United - revealing how one former player tried to secretly record a confession from his abuser. Eventually, the three separate cases are brought to trial, but the question remains - why did it take so long to reach this point?

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: After an arduous six-week journey, the search for the country's best amateur cook reaches its climax. The finalists have to push themselves to the limit for one last time before judges John Torode and Gregg Wallace crown one of the three talented cooks MasterChef Champion 2021. Tonight, they must produce the best three dishes of their lives, push their culinary boundaries and produce jaw-droppingly impressive food for the judges

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the evening session on day four at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, featuring the concluding session of Mark Allen's first-round match against Lyu Haotian and the opening frames of Barry Hawkins' contest against Matthew Selt. Allen has participated in this event in each of the last 14 years, but his best showing remains his semi-final appearance in 2009, while 'The Hawk' was runner-up to Ronnie O'Sullivan in 2013

programme.title: A303: Highway to the Sun
programme.desc: Tom Fort travels along the 92-mile-long A303 from the Hampshire town of Basingstoke to Honiton in Devon. Originally designed in the 1960s, the route has been the subject of many proposed upgrades - several of which were strongly opposed due to their potential impact on sites of historic and natural significance, including Stonehenge. The programme charts the highway's turbulent history, as well as examining the development of the region over the past 5,000 years

programme.title: Stephen: The Murder That Changed a Nation
programme.desc: The second police investigation reveals shocking surveillance footage of the gang alleged to have killed Stephen, and with the help of their lawyer Imran Khan, Doreen and Neville Lawrence launch the first private murder prosecution in 150 years. However, when it collapses, questions about the failure of the first police investigation mount

programme.title: Lights Up
programme.desc: Adaptation of the National Theatre of Scotland's award-winning stage play about the life of a young transgender man, inspired by the life of Adam Kashmiry, who also takes the lead role. Born in Egypt, Adam was classed as female at birth but always knew he was male. Trapped in the wrong body, in a deeply conservative society, he knew he had to escape. What followed was beyond Adam's wildest dreams. Written by Frances Poet

programme.title: Colour: The Spectrum of Science
programme.desc: Early Earth was the canvas for a vast new palette, with the diversity of human skin tones telling the story of how humanity spread and ultimately conquered the planet. Dr Helen Czerski explores the true masters of colour - which are often the smallest and most elusive - travelling to the mountains of Tennessee to witness the colourful mating display of fireflies, and revealing the marine creatures that can change the hue of their skin in order to hide from the world

programme.title: David Stratton's Stories of Australian Cinema
programme.desc: How Australian cinema celebrates the endurance of outsiders, whether they are newcomers to a strange new land in They're a Weird Mob and Wake in Fright, or locals out of step with the mainstream in Evil Angels, Muriel's Wedding and Priscilla, Queen of the Desert

programme.title: A303: Highway to the Sun
programme.desc: Tom Fort travels along the 92-mile-long A303 from the Hampshire town of Basingstoke to Honiton in Devon. Originally designed in the 1960s, the route has been the subject of many proposed upgrades - several of which were strongly opposed due to their potential impact on sites of historic and natural significance, including Stonehenge. The programme charts the highway's turbulent history, as well as examining the development of the region over the past 5,000 years

programme.title: Stephen: The Murder That Changed a Nation
programme.desc: The second police investigation reveals shocking surveillance footage of the gang alleged to have killed Stephen, and with the help of their lawyer Imran Khan, Doreen and Neville Lawrence launch the first private murder prosecution in 150 years. However, when it collapses, questions about the failure of the first police investigation mount

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the evening session on day five at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, featuring the concluding sessions of Mark Williams v Sam Craigie and Barry Hawkins v Matthew Selt. Williams prevailed 10-5 against Alan McManus at this stage last year, while Hawkins' greater experience showed in a comfortable 10-2 victory over Alexander Ursenbacher

programme.title: Watergate
programme.desc: The documentary reveals how Richard Nixon had the burglars paid off to keep silent, allowing him to win a second presidential term in 1972. But when the facts began to emerge, he sought a succession of scapegoats - although the fact that his office had been recorded meant his crimes were no longer a secret

programme.title: Stephen: The Murder That Changed a Nation
programme.desc: Final part of the documentary exploring Stephen Lawrence's murder and the subsequent police investigation. The public inquiry leads to a change in the law meaning the suspects can be retried. Detective Clive Driscoll takes on the case, and with the help of advances in forensic science he arrests Gary Dobson and David Norris. But as justice seems to have finally been done, revelations that the police spied on the Lawrence family begin to surface

programme.title: Lights Up
programme.desc: Rapper Andy Brooks - also known as Testament - presents this modern-day interpretation of the Orpheus legend, fusing spoken word and beatboxing with a cinematic score performed by members of the Orchestra and Chorus of Opera North. Orpheus is alone, playing tunes in his record shop, when an enigmatic stranger sets his fragile reality into a spin, forcing him to confront some painful truths from the past. Testament takes the lead role, with support from mezzo Helen Evora and Everal A Walsh

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: Documentary about a fraud scandal in Romania's healthcare system that was exposed after survivors of a nightclub fire began dying in suspicious circumstances while recovering in hospital with non-life-threatening injuries. When a new health minister was subsequently appointed, journalists follow his attempts, in the face of monumental obstacles, to reform a system riddled with corruption

programme.title: How to Make
programme.desc: Designer Zoe Laughlin sets out to redesign audio headphones, an intimate item that transport the wearer to another place. In search of inspiration she heads to Hull University to discover a new substance that can turn any surface into a speaker - from a wall to a table and even the bone of a skull. Zoe also looks at the device's history, from the stethoscope-style home contraptions of the 19th century and the game-changing 1980s Sony Walkman. But what will she come up with in her own design?

programme.title: Stephen: The Murder That Changed a Nation
programme.desc: Final part of the documentary exploring Stephen Lawrence's murder and the subsequent police investigation. The public inquiry leads to a change in the law meaning the suspects can be retried. Detective Clive Driscoll takes on the case, and with the help of advances in forensic science he arrests Gary Dobson and David Norris. But as justice seems to have finally been done, revelations that the police spied on the Lawrence family begin to surface

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the evening session on day six at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, featuring the concluding session of Mark Selby's first-round match against Kurt Maflin and the opening frames of the second-round contest between Neil Robertson and Jack Lisowski or Ali Carter. Three-time champion Selby prevailed 10-6 over Jordan Brown in round one last year and went on to reach the semi-finals, where he was eventually defeated 17-16 by Ronnie O'Sullivan

programme.title: Hunting the Nazi Gold Train
programme.desc: In August 2015 two amateur researchers declared they had found a lost Nazi train buried in a railway cutting near Walbrzych in Silesia, southern Poland. This followed decades of local folklore that a train, loaded with gold, had vanished after leaving Wroclaw in early 1945. Dan Snow follows the treasure hunters as they embark on the hunt, while also investigating this extraordinary story that leads all the way to Adolf Hitler himself and his secret plans for this remote corner of the Third Reich during the closing months of the Second World War

programme.title: The Man with the Iron Heart (2017)
programme.desc: In 1942, two Czech resistance members prepare for a suicide mission against the Third Reich as they plan to assassinate Nazi leader Reinhard Heydrich, the mastermind of the Final Solution. Thriller, starring Jason Clarke, Rosamund Pike, Jack O'Connell and Jack Reynor

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: In a documentary made when she was 105, Brunhilde Pomsel talks about her time as the secretary and stenographer to Nazi propaganda minister Joseph Goebbels. Reflecting on her life, she makes it clear that the dangers that led to the rise of fascism have not been overcome

programme.title: A History of Art in Three Colours
programme.desc: Art historian James Fox follows up his British Masters series by investigating artists' use of blue, white and gold, and explains how, by choosing these colours, painters have stirred emotions, changed the way people behave and had an impact on the course of history. He begins with gold and its use in early sun worship, before reinterpreting the treasures of the pharaohs, the mosaics of Byzantium and the work of Renaissance master Benvenuto Cellini. He also explores Gustav Klimt's The Kiss and discovers a Birmingham inventor who tried to achieve the alchemist's dream - turning base metals into gold

programme.title: Hunting the Nazi Gold Train
programme.desc: In August 2015 two amateur researchers declared they had found a lost Nazi train buried in a railway cutting near Walbrzych in Silesia, southern Poland. This followed decades of local folklore that a train, loaded with gold, had vanished after leaving Wroclaw in early 1945. Dan Snow follows the treasure hunters as they embark on the hunt, while also investigating this extraordinary story that leads all the way to Adolf Hitler himself and his secret plans for this remote corner of the Third Reich during the closing months of the Second World War

programme.title: Love Monster
programme.desc: Elder Panda is teaching Love Monster to fly a kite. Love Monster loves to watch it swish around in the air. Elder Panda goes off to get some froyo, which is perfect on Kite Day. Before he goes, he tells Love Monster that soon it will be time for him to go on to his next kiting challenge. Love Monster looks at all the other kites and sees the dragon kite, which is the largest kite of them all. Love Monster’s small kite doesn’t seem that exciting any more. He decides to try the dragon kite for just a minute. At first it seems easy, but then a wind gets it tangled in a tree. The rope breaks, and the kite flies off, causing mayhem in Fluffytown with everyone thinking it is a real dragon invasion. Finally, Elder Panda arrives and manages to bring it down to the ground using an umbrella, and Love Monster comes clean, saying how sorry he is. Elder Panda is disappointed but he does understand, as he too wanted to fly before he could run, but sometimes you have to start small before going big.

Parents, carers and their little ones at home are invited to join in with all the fun activities too! Inside Giovanna’s bag is a night light, and all the parents and babies have different objects and toys that could be used as part of a bedtime routine. These are explored through discovery, play, song and story. Grown-ups at home are encouraged to take part by hiding safe, baby-appropriate bedtime objects and toys inside a fabric bag or pillowcase for them to reveal and explore with their baby! The baby lullaby Little One Time For Bed features, as does a story involving Baby Bear, and viewers at home show what they’ve been doing at bedtime too. The Baby Bear Song gets everyone moving about, before some calm time with sensory lights and the Goodbye Song.

But after accidentally knocking into Delivery Duckling, the cards get mixed up, and he sends out Elder Kitten's envelope without his card in it! Love Monster must chase down Delivery Duckling and stop her from making the delivery.

programme.title: Dog Loves Books
programme.desc: Dog wants some high adventure, so he and Pug visit several different stories. Each time they arrive in their new story, they find that they are being chased by various characters. Mrs Honey Bunny comes to their rescue, chasing away all the chasers.

programme.title: My Very First
programme.desc: When Henry and his family go on a trip to a nearby pond, it’s fishing nets at the ready as they try their hand at a spot of shrimping! It’s not easy as the tiny shrimp are good at hiding. Luckily, Henry comes up with a plan – but will it work? And will they catch any shrimp? Later, it’s Henry who is good at hiding in a family game of hide-and-seek. But will his clever hiding place be discovered?

programme.title: Andy's Prehistoric Adventures

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: Grandmaster Glitch has taken his teddy, Cuddles, to Ilulissat in Greenland to show him the sights, but Cuddles falls out of Grim HQ and gets stuck in the snow. The Grimbots melt the ice around Cuddles, and Glitch then uses Grim HQ to pull up the massive chunk of ice and carry it into the air. The Go Jetters are also in Ilulissat trying to spot some whales. They learn that Ilulissat means iceberg and that an iceberg is a huge chunk of ice which forms on land and then breaks off into the sea. Just then, Glitch's heavy iceberg falls from Grim HQ and starts sliding down the hill towards the town. The Go Jetters need to stop it and protect the town!

programme.title: Bing

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The Squirrels are playing outside in the snow and decide to build a jolly-looking snowman. However, the fun begins when the punky penguins turn up

programme.title: Go Jetters

programme.title: Love Monster
programme.desc: It’s Tidy Up Day, so everyone in Fluffytown is tidying things. Love Monster helps Book Cub to clean up the forest, and as he does so, finds several items that would make great souvenirs. These include a bunny-shaped rock, a cloud-shaped leaf and a heart-shaped stone. But he doesn't notice that each time he take a souvenir, it disturbs a forest creature that was using it. Once he finally realises this, he and Book Cub work together to put all of the treasures back in their rightful places.

programme.title: School of Roars

programme.title: Raa Raa the Noisy Lion

programme.title: Yakka Dee!
programme.desc: Dee encourages a friend to say ‘van’, and we go on a whirlwind word adventure. We see lots of different vans – a yellow van, a white van, a big van and even a veggie van. There’s a mini van, a flower van, a fast van and a camper van. There’s an ice cream van, a post van and a loaded-up van! The journey is repeated with some special surprises along the way, and everyone has a boogie when Dee’s friend finally succeeds in saying today’s word!

programme.desc: Educational programme for children aged between four and seven

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Tinky Winky, Dipsy and Laa-Laa follow a trail of muddy footprints and find Po in bed with very muddy feet. The Noo-noo cleans off the mud

programme.title: The Baby Club
programme.desc: Giovanna and Baby Bear welcome babies Reau, Alfie, Charlie, Ollie, Otis, River and their grown-ups to The Baby Club. Parents, carers and their little ones at home are invited to join in with all the fun activities too! Inside Giovanna’s bag is a night light, and all the parents and babies have different objects and toys that could be used as part of a bedtime routine. These are explored through discovery, play, song and story. Grown-ups at home are encouraged to take part by hiding safe, baby-appropriate bedtime objects and toys inside a fabric bag or pillowcase for them to reveal and explore with their baby! The baby lullaby Little One Time For Bed features, as does a story involving Baby Bear, and viewers at home show what they’ve been doing at bedtime too. The Baby Bear Song gets everyone moving about, before some calm time with sensory lights and the Goodbye Song.

programme.title: Hey Duggee

programme.title: B.O.T and the Beasties
programme.desc: The Shuffle Snuffler sniffs its way through the jungle. It is horrified when it smells B.O.T.! Can B.O.T. avoid being sneezed to pieces and complete this mission?

programme.title: Garden Tales
programme.desc: Pigs Thelma and Louise have heard about the field at the top of the garden with great views and good food. It's a bit of a trek to get there, and Louise can’t possibly make it without putting her trotters up for forty winks. Parker the Pheasant can’t wake her up, so Auntie the Tawny Owl has to get involved. Does Louise make it?

programme.title: Let's Go for a Walk

programme.title: Colours
programme.desc: Series for young children teaching them about colour. In this episode, we learn about objects associated with the colour green, from grass to grapes. We explore the meaning of being green, and the episode concludes with a specially written poem summarising the colour green.

programme.title: Patchwork Pals
programme.desc: Animation following the adventure of a group of animals living on a patchwork blanket

programme.title: My Very First
programme.desc: Join 13-month-old Gia and twins, Chester and Elias, as they discover the world around them and find out what words they are learning. Both families are playing at home, talking about colours and a four-legged friend. They all head to baby classes, where they use music to practice their words, and some puppets come along to help them learn! Back at home, Chester and Elias play in the garden with their brother, going up and down the slide. Gia takes mum and granddad for a walk in the woods, where they see lots of beautiful things - they even find a bird! Finally, Chester and Elias play a counting game and Gia makes a wonderful word wall in the garden using pictures of things they saw that day. Remember, every child develops differently.

Dr Piggles asks Love Monster if he can fill in for him while he rests, assuring him that he'll be right there to help out. But when the hiccups get worse, he has to lock himself in the back room, and Love Monster must find ways to make all the patients laugh by himself.

programme.title: Dog Loves Books
programme.desc: Dog and Pug go an adventure underground where they meet Granny Pug. They slide down a tunnel to the centre of the earth. When they arrive, they find some very friendly dinosaurs. Granny Pug promises to keep the dinosaur’s home a secret.

programme.desc: Iona and Elba make pancakes for a fabulous family breakfast. There’s lots of egg cracking, whisking, and fruit chopping, but will Iona manage a successful pancake flip? Later, the sisters build a car for their teddy bear - beep beep! And Iona draws a family tree with Mum and learns all about her relatives, old and new.

programme.desc: Louis feels trapped as Vicky's blackmailing escalates, and he wonders how much longer he can keep his double life a secret. programme.desc: Comedian Tudur Owen presents a selection of small-screen clips featuring north Wales - a land of weekend warriors, Klingon speakers and eccentric railwaymen

programme.title: The Syndicate
programme.desc: The friends desperately rush Roxy to hospital but are horrified to discover that emergency medical treatment is hugely expensive. Unless they can get the money to pay it off, Roxy won't be allowed to leave the country. In all the confusion, Keeley realises they have missed out on their kidnap plan. Time is running out for the syndicate - and they are still no closer to getting proof. Unknown to them, however, Frank is confronted by Mr Shriver, the head of Mercury Millions' fraud team. Does he know about the stolen ticket?

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Norman questions Norma's faith in him, and Romero's presented with evidence that may change the course of Miss Watson's murder investigation. programme.title: Our Food, Our Family with Michela Chiappa
programme.desc: Born in 1962 to Nigerian parents studying medicine in London, Kemi Nevins now runs a successful cafe in Abercynon serving up delicious food. However, none of that she offers reflects her Nigerian background. Now Kemi's children are adults themselves, they would like to know more about their heritage, so the Welsh-Italian cook visits Kemi to help her reconnect to her roots through her love of food

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: A Gloucestershire woman brings in her King Charles II painting, hoping Lucia Scalisi can restore it to its crowning glory, and silversmith Brenton West works on a well-travelled trunk, which holds cherished memories for a mother and daughter. Goldsmith Richard Talman tries to reunite the broken pieces of an engagement ring and toy restorers Julie and Amanda revive a toy donkey that comforted a young child after he lost his mother and his sister

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: It's summer week in the sewing room as the contestants are challenged to create three garments too look and feel good in the heat. They begin by following the pattern for a pair of paperbag shorts, before transforming second-hand men's swimming gear into a women's cocktail outfit. The made-to-measure challenge sees them taking on button-down sundresses, and eagle-eyed judges Patrick Grant and Esme Young will be looking for perfectly aligned buttonholes and secure fastenings. Who will miss the mark and be second to leave? Joe Lycett has the answer

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Highlights of the latest Premier League fixtures, featuring Tottenham Hotspur v Southampton at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium and Aston Villa v Manchester City at Villa Park. Spurs were looking to boost their European football aspirations by emulating their impressive 5-2 victory in the reverse fixture, while City looked to move a step closer to the title. Plus, a look back at last night's contest between Chelsea and Brighton & Hove Albion

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, and a look at the biggest issues in the women's game. Arsenal are the defending champions, but face stiff competition from the likes of Chelsea, Manchester City and Manchester United

programme.title: I Can See Your Voice

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: In Glasgow, ambulance crew Gary and Stephen are called to help a patient with a rare autoimmune disease who has lost mobility. programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson is in Derbyshire with a recently married couple making an epic location shift, swapping the Blue Mountains in Australia for the wilds of the Peak District. programme.title: Money for Nothing

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Upholsterer Sonnaz Nooranvary revamps a family treasure with an intriguing history - a threadbare theatre chair that once played host to thousands of theatre-goers and bingo players in the auditorium of the Derby Hippodrome. A rare timepiece that has not worked for years and a cherished bronze locket also receive attention

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Patrick opens up to Isaac about his brother Paul, and how he wishes he could have opened up about the trouble he was in - the trouble that eventually got him killed. Billy and Honey discover that Amy has been posting nasty things about Janet on social media and Bernie overhears Rainie and Stuart talking about finding a surrogate again

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Industry titans Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden, Touker Suleyman, Tej Lalvani and Sara Davies are entertained by a DJ and dancer pitching a socially distanced nightclub business, while an Irish inventor with a muscle massaging device hopes his proposition will loosen up the Dragons' pockets. A pair of optical consultants have their sights set on investment with their ready-readers business and a 29-year-old tech enthusiast is confident that his clever way of monetising people's personal online data. Presented by Evan Davis

programme.title: This Is My House
programme.desc: Joe Sugg and Richard Madeley join regular panellists Bill Bailey, Emily Atack and Judi Love in trying to guess which of the four Clares is the real owner of a property in Guildford, Surrey. Those trying to pass themselves off as the lady of the manor include a former air hostess and a 'Klara' from Slovakia, who says most people think she is the cleaner not the homeowner. Presented by Stacey Dooley

programme.title: Greta Thunberg: A Year to Change the World
programme.desc: Greta sets off to the annual World Economic Forum in Davos, Switzerland, to call for an immediate reduction in fossil fuel use, but is frustrated by what she sees as a media obsession constantly pitting her against Donald Trump. As she travels across Europe, Greta meets Polish miners who fear that climate change policies will further decimate their industry and culture, and discovers the prototype of a technology that aims to capture carbon dioxide from factory emissions before it enters the atmosphere. She also meets one of her inspirations - Sir David Attenborough

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Michael Ball present the live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: A frustrated Nancy tries to track down a lost package, leaving her family curious over what could be so important - especially when she refuses to look after Ollie. Isaac has questions about his family, and Bernie is surprised when Molly apologises and asks her out for a coffee

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Louis feels trapped as Vicky's blackmailing escalates, and he wonders how much longer he can keep his double life a secret. Max grows more determined to catch the hospital thief, a desperate Dom campaigns for Ange to assist with his operation and Kian leads Lucky on a merry dance across the hospital as he tries to dodge a drugs test

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: Patients from Britain's most remote community - on Fair Isle, south of Shetland - wait for a weather window to make the trip to Lerwick for important operations

programme.title: The Syndicate
programme.desc: The friends desperately rush Roxy to hospital but are horrified to discover that emergency medical treatment is hugely expensive. Unless they can get the money to pay it off, Roxy won't be allowed to leave the country. In all the confusion, Keeley realises they have missed out on their kidnap plan. Time is running out for the syndicate - and they are still no closer to getting proof. Unknown to them, however, Frank is confronted by Mr Shriver, the head of Mercury Millions' fraud team. Does he know about the stolen ticket?

programme.title: The Campaign
programme.desc: Rebecca Curran and Martin Geissler present the latest news and analysis from the election campaign

programme.title: Glow Up: Britain's Next Make-Up Star
programme.desc: The contest for aspiring make-up artists returns, with new host Maya Jama. The contestants' first professional assignment sees them creating an eye-catching look for a Superdrug beauty campaign. In the creative brief, judges Val Garland and Dominic Skinner ask the artists to show what makes them unique. Only then do the judges decide which two will take on the first elimination face-off

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Norman questions Norma's faith in him, and Romero's presented with evidence that may change the course of Miss Watson's murder investigation. Meanwhile, Zane's thirst for revenge threatens Dylan. A prequel to Alfred Hitchcock's Psycho, based on Robert Bloch's novel of the same name, depicting the life of Norman Bates and his mother Norma prior to the events portrayed in the film

programme.title: River City
programme.desc: When Paul is found to be a kidney donor match for Rory, Alex seeks advice from Eve and makes a shocking discovery. Bob's suspicions of Rebecca cause her to jeopardise her relationship with Angus, Stevie's attempts to find an income fail, while Ruby's concern for Maggie grows

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: A Gloucestershire woman brings in her King Charles II painting, hoping Lucia Scalisi can restore it to its crowning glory, and silversmith Brenton West works on a well-travelled trunk, which holds cherished memories for a mother and daughter. Goldsmith Richard Talman tries to reunite the broken pieces of an engagement ring and toy restorers Julie and Amanda revive a toy donkey that comforted a young child after he lost his mother and his sister

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: It's summer week in the sewing room as the contestants are challenged to create three garments too look and feel good in the heat. They begin by following the pattern for a pair of paperbag shorts, before transforming second-hand men's swimming gear into a women's cocktail outfit. The made-to-measure challenge sees them taking on button-down sundresses, and eagle-eyed judges Patrick Grant and Esme Young will be looking for perfectly aligned buttonholes and secure fastenings. Who will miss the mark and be second to leave? Joe Lycett has the answer

programme.title: Sportscene
programme.desc: Steven Thompson brings you action and reaction to the evening's six Premiership matches, featuring St Johnstone v Rangers at McDiarmid Park, Aberdeen v Celtic at Pittodrie, Hibernian v Livingstone at Easter Road, Hamilton Academical v Motherwell at Fountain of Youth Stadium, Kilmarnock v Dundee United at Rugby Park and Ross County v St Mirren at Global Energy Stadium

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Highlights of the latest Premier League fixtures, featuring Tottenham Hotspur v Southampton at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium and Aston Villa v Manchester City at Villa Park. Spurs were looking to boost their European football aspirations by emulating their impressive 5-2 victory in the reverse fixture, while City looked to move a step closer to the title. Plus, a look back at last night's contest between Chelsea and Brighton & Hove Albion

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, and a look at the biggest issues in the women's game. Arsenal are the defending champions, but face stiff competition from the likes of Chelsea, Manchester City and Manchester United

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, and a look at the biggest issues in the women's game. Arsenal are the defending champions, but face stiff competition from the likes of Chelsea, Manchester City and Manchester United

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Norma refuses to tell Norman the truth about his blackouts, while Romero digs deeper into the death of Miss Watson. A prequel to Alfred Hitchcock's Psycho, based on Robert Bloch's novel of the same name, depicting the life of Norman Bates and his mother Norma prior to the events portrayed in the film

programme.title: I Can See Your Voice
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness hosts the mystery singing game show, in which contestants aided by a celebrity panel must spot good singers without hearing them. Regular celebrity investigators Jimmy Carr, Alison Hammond and Amanda Holden are joined by guest Nadine Coyle, as they assist two contestants from London in picking out the best singers and winning up to £10,000

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The team visits a couple who bought a prefabricated bungalow in Chesterton, Staffordshire, as well as first-time developers in Sutton in Surrey and Bilston in the West Midlands. Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell present

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: A fraudster feigns innocence when caught scamming a holiday company. Elsewhere, CCTV shows that an employee claiming compensation was actually drunk when he fell

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: In Glasgow, ambulance crew Gary and Stephen are called to help a patient with a rare autoimmune disease who has lost mobility. In Edinburgh, Moira and Blair treat a young man with severe abdominal pain. And there is a serious car crash just outside the city. In Aberdeen, paramedics Ewan and Alan give emergency treatment to a lady whose heartbeat is dangerously fast and rush her to hospital

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles presents from Newbury, Berkshire, with experts Caroline Hawley and David Harper ready to help the blue and red teams maximise their profit at auction. The presenter also recalls the world's largest peace camp

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Karen intervenes when a bitterly divorced couple arrive for appointments at the same time. Medical drama series set in a busy Midlands surgery, with Jan Pearson

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson is in Derbyshire with a recently married couple making an epic location shift, swapping the Blue Mountains in Australia for the wilds of the Peak District. They have a significant budget of £750,000 and have their hearts set on a character home with a large garden for their three dogs. Jules also encounters an unlikely centre of manufacturing nestled in the county's rural countryside

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore enlists the help of expert furniture artist Chloe Kempster to set to work on a reclaimed chest of drawers, while Guy Trench needs to put his best foot forward with some dated shoe lasts. Sarah sets herself the challenge of repurposing some rusty hanging baskets

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Upholsterer Sonnaz Nooranvary revamps a family treasure with an intriguing history - a threadbare theatre chair that once played host to thousands of theatre-goers and bingo players in the auditorium of the Derby Hippodrome. A rare timepiece that has not worked for years and a cherished bronze locket also receive attention

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones hosts the live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Patrick opens up to Isaac about his brother Paul, and how he wishes he could have opened up about the trouble he was in - the trouble that eventually got him killed. Billy and Honey discover that Amy has been posting nasty things about Janet on social media and Bernie overhears Rainie and Stuart talking about finding a surrogate again

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Industry titans Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden, Touker Suleyman, Tej Lalvani and Sara Davies are entertained by a DJ and dancer pitching a socially distanced nightclub business, while an Irish inventor with a muscle massaging device hopes his proposition will loosen up the Dragons' pockets. A pair of optical consultants have their sights set on investment with their ready-readers business and a 29-year-old tech enthusiast is confident that his clever way of monetising people's personal online data. Presented by Evan Davis

programme.title: This Is My House
programme.desc: Joe Sugg and Richard Madeley join regular panellists Bill Bailey, Emily Atack and Judi Love in trying to guess which of the four Clares is the real owner of a property in Guildford, Surrey. Those trying to pass themselves off as the lady of the manor include a former air hostess and a 'Klara' from Slovakia, who says most people think she is the cleaner not the homeowner. Presented by Stacey Dooley

programme.title: The View
programme.desc: Mark Carruthers reviews the week's political news, including reports on the latest events at Stormont and Westminster. Plus, comment and analysis with studio guests and an alternative view on recent political headlines

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate, inviting a panel of politicians and other guests to answer questions from a virtual audience on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Adam Fleming, Laura Kuenssberg and Chris Mason discuss the week's biggest stories as they round-up recent events in Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Greta Thunberg: A Year to Change the World
programme.desc: Greta sets off to the annual World Economic Forum in Davos, Switzerland, to call for an immediate reduction in fossil fuel use, but is frustrated by what she sees as a media obsession constantly pitting her against Donald Trump. As she travels across Europe, Greta meets Polish miners who fear that climate change policies will further decimate their industry and culture, and discovers the prototype of a technology that aims to capture carbon dioxide from factory emissions before it enters the atmosphere. She also meets one of her inspirations - Sir David Attenborough

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Clive Myrie investigates allegations of racism in the Church of England. A year after the archbishop of Canterbury, Justin Welby, admitted that the Church was still 'deeply institutionally racist', Clive meets vicars, curates and theological trainees to understand the scale of the problem. He hears stories of racist abuse and claims of a culture that creates a hostile environment for Christians of colour, who have suffered in silence for fear of further discrimination or losing their jobs
