programme.desc: Another selection of B&B owners take it in turns as hosts to decide which of their establishments provides the best value for money. The contest begins at Edenmore Guest House in Ardrossan, Ayrshire, where things start well for owner Hugh Begbie when his guests soon make themselves at home. However, a game of curling soon brings out their competitive sides, and a rusty pole causes a stir

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competitors are invited to Grassington Lodge in the market town of Grassington in the Yorkshire Dales, where owner Diane Lowe believes her establishment will impress with its 'little touches'. The guests enjoy their eggs Benedict breakfast and Diane receives some kind comments, but fellow proprietor Hugh takes some convincing that she is not a snob

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Jeanette and Andrew Collette open the doors to Manor Farm in Oxfordshire, where the guests seem impressed on arrival - but the peaceful, rural feel doesn't appeal to Elle and Ian Betchley, and self-confessed 'dour' Scotsman Hugh Begbie just wants to be left alone without Jeanette's fussing

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Ian and Elle Betchley, owners of the George and Dragon Hotel in Princes Risborough, Buckinghamshire, welcome the contestants to their B&B, but some of them are not impressed to discover a stained teapot and dirty rooms. The duo organise an afternoon of ghost-hunting at the nearby Hellfire Caves, however there is a lack of paranormal activity. At breakfast, Diane and Sandra do not rate the eggs Benedict, leaving the hosts with mixed feedback at check-out time

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners confront their rivals and discover what they have been paid, and emotions run high as the winner is announced. The host of Edenmore Guest House, Hugh Begbie, deals with criticism of the state of his exterior, Jeanette and Andrew from Manor Farm in Oxfordshire dejectedly analyse why their newfound friends would not stay again, and Elle and Ian Betchley from the George and Dragon in Buckinghamshire tackle Hugh on his hosting score

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Simon O'Brien turns tank parts into garden furniture, while Henry Cole takes his life in his hands with a motorised skateboard

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Chester's 18 chimpanzees face upheaval when new female chimp Vila arrives from a zoo in Germany. Meanwhile, nine-year-old cheetah mum Kinky Tail is vomiting after meals, and her three-year-old daughter Safi must watch and wait as her mum is taken to the zoo's medical centre. Cameras also follow Asian short-clawed otter Annie, who recently gave birth to a large litter of five pups

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Billy and Gwendoline rope in Dick to help them put up a chandelier, and then they have to track down some wild boars that are causing chaos in and around the chateau. Clive and Tanith attempt to make a fully self-sufficient dish for their restaurant. Meanwhile at Paul and Karen's chateau, Paul continues the task of converting their water tower into an office - in 33-degree heat

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud meets Andy Stakes and Jeanette Hardy, who are building a new house on top of a sheer cliff on the west coast of Scotland. The pair want to spend £250,000 on a two-bedroom, glass-fronted and earth-sheltered home for themselves and their two beloved Newfoundland dogs. However, construction on such an exposed site is fraught with danger and the pair face no end of problems throughout the build, and a last minute design change that begs the biggest question: even if Andy finishes the house, will Jeanette actually like it?

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Fuzz Townshend and Tim Shaw take on the restoration of one of the greatest British post-war sports cars ever built - a 1961 Austin Healey 3000

programme.title: Prince Philip: God of Our Island
programme.desc: Documentary about the tribesmen of Tanna - a remote island in the South Pacific - who worship Philip as a God. They believe that after his death, the prince's spirit will return home to their island, and bring peace and harmony to the world. The film follows their five ambassadors - who came to the UK to meet the Duke in 2007 - on a pilgrimage around their island as they prepare for the prince's return

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A 22-year-old cyclist is rushed to St George's Hospital after he collided with a car on the way to work and 'bullseyed' its rear window. Since he was not wearing a helmet, doctors send him for a CT scan to assess whether he has sustained a serious head injury, or life-changing damage to his neck, spine or internal organs. Meanwhile, 91-year-old Peter arrives at A&E three nights after suffering a fall at home, and 26-year-old Emma needs treatment for a head wound sustained during an epileptic seizure

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy words-and-numbers quiz, with Sean Lock and Roisin Conaty taking on guest captain Alan Carr and Joe Lycett. Susie Dent is joined by Spencer Jones in Dictionary Corner, while Rachel Riley looks after the numbers and letters

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Fuzz Townshend and Tim Shaw take on the restoration of one of the greatest British post-war sports cars ever built - a 1961 Austin Healey 3000

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A 22-year-old cyclist is rushed to St George's Hospital after he collided with a car on the way to work and 'bullseyed' its rear window. Since he was not wearing a helmet, doctors send him for a CT scan to assess whether he has sustained a serious head injury, or life-changing damage to his neck, spine or internal organs. Meanwhile, 91-year-old Peter arrives at A&E three nights after suffering a fall at home, and 26-year-old Emma needs treatment for a head wound sustained during an epileptic seizure

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty heads to Thailand to find out about the surprising that makes a prawn cracker unique, while Matt Tebbutt visits Britain's largest producer of fresh herbs to learn about a new hi-tech method of extending shelf-life. Kate Quilton investigates what actually goes into sodium-replacement salts and examines whether salt really deserves its unhealthy reputation

programme.title: Kirstie's Vintage Gems
programme.desc: Master sculptor and gilder Adam Williamson teaches Kirstie Allsop how to apply gold leaf to a second-hand picture frame to give it a luxury look and feel

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin helps Sandra and Peter Dodsworth buy a retirement property in the picturesque south Orlando region of Florida

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien travel across the UK to find unique and valuable items, and their latest find is an old bureau which gets a makeover in Yorkshire

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Simon O'Brien recycles some historic oak while Henry Cole comes unstuck when he tries to get to grips with an old Ford Escort

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: The probate researchers look for the relatives of a woman who died without leaving a will, and discover that her husband was a Second World War meteorologist. Plus, how an heir hunt that went all the way to Australia reveals the story of mass emigration thanks to the 'ten pound pom' campaign

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: Charlie Luxton meets a Cambridgeshire couple with plans to build a four-bedroom cottage, whose budget has turned out to be less accommodating than originally planned for

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition begins at Langsmeade House in Thame, Oxfordshire, where Dutch-born owner Marianne Abben and her housekeeper Fran Birch hope to demonstrate that their B&B offers the best value for money. The quirky pair's hosting style proves a hit with some of their guests, before others reveal their competitive side during a Charleston dance lesson

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The owners visit the Stratford House Guesthouse in Stratford-upon-Avon on their second outing, where couple Natasha and Dai Pollock hope to impress their peers. But things do not run smoothly, as the guests are left unimpressed with the decor and discover nasty surprises in some of the drawers

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third B&B of the week is pet-friendly Edmund House in Deal, Kent, where owner Michael Prime is joined by his mum Lorna. On arrival, Marianne Abben and Fran Birch love the seaside-inspired room, but entrepreneur Mark Genders is far from thrilled by the 'no frills' experience. Over dinner, tensions run high after self-confessed trouble-maker Marianne asks Mark to reveal which of the group he would and would not employ, and Lorna gets short shrift when she puts forward her application

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Entrepreneur Mark Genders welcomes the contestants to the Five Bells Pub in Berkshire and hopes they will approve of his small but perfectly formed rooms. He organises an ale-tasting activity, although it fails to please all of his guests. Michael and Lorna admit they have seen a new side of Mark - however, will it affect their feedback?

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners settle some scores and discover how much they have been paid, before the winner is revealed. Marianne and Fran from Langsmeade House begin by explaining that their worn rug is in fact a 200-year-old antique, Natasha and Dal from Stratford House confront Michael and Lorna about their strong language and cleaning techniques, while Mark Genders compares his experience at Edmund House to flying with a low budget airline

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Simon O'Brien converts a lantern into a stylish table and Henry Cole changes a fire extinguisher into a steampunk lamp as the pair visit a private railway

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: An elephant calf is born at Chester Zoo, but its young half-sister doesn't welcome the new arrival. Meanwhile, bush dog dad Franco is under pressure as its family grows, and an onager - a rare type of wild horse - faces a major operation

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: At Billy and Gwendoline's chateau, Billy and his father try to level a patio so it can pass health and safety regulations. As Paul and Karen struggle to get the office conversion finished in time, a huge damaged pine tree needs to be cut down. While Clive's dad Martin attempts to fix a wall surrounding the lake, Clive investigates a huge, creepy nest in the roof of the orangery, the building that he hopes will one day be a house for his father

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud meets entrepreneur Paul Wilkinson and his wife Amy, who are building a giant new home on a 16-acre plot in Lincolnshire. The superstructure will be comprised of five circular buildings made up of interlocking timber cylinders, linked by raised wooden walkways that hang over a fishing lake. The complicated build proves difficult for Paul's local team, and their ambitious schedule of completing the project in a year soon looks out of reach

programme.title: Matt Baker: Our Farm in the Dales
programme.desc: The ancient meadows get some new inhabitants when the family take delivery of some new beehives, while Matt prepares for lambing season and selects a ram with help from mum Janice. Billy the vet returns to check on the puppies before they leave for their forever homes, and the family reflects on the positive changes they have made since Janice's accident

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy words-and-numbers quiz, with team captain Sean Lock and Johnny Vegas taking on guest captain Alan Carr and Lou Sanders. Lexicographer Susie Dent is joined by the Brett Domino Trio to adjudicate in Dictionary Corner, while Rachel Riley looks after the numbers and letters

programme.title: Jon Snow's Very Hard Questions
programme.desc: In the grand final, the two highest scoring teams from the qualifying rounds, Singers on Buzzers and Ein-Steins, go head to head as they strive to win the £5000 prize money

programme.title: Prince: Last Year of a Legend
programme.desc: Documentary charting the key events of Prince's final 12 months, featuring contributions from friends and fellow music stars including George Clinton and CeeLo Green. It begins with the Rally 4 Peace concert for riot-torn Baltimore in April 2015, which was followed by a secret gig at the White House for the Obamas and guests, two new albums, and the Piano & A Microphone tour, where he played solo. The programme then looks into what led to the musician's unexpected death at his Paisley Park home in April 2016

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy words-and-numbers quiz, with team captain Sean Lock and Johnny Vegas taking on guest captain Alan Carr and Lou Sanders. Lexicographer Susie Dent is joined by the Brett Domino Trio to adjudicate in Dictionary Corner, while Rachel Riley looks after the numbers and letters

programme.title: Matt Baker: Our Farm in the Dales
programme.desc: The ancient meadows get some new inhabitants when the family take delivery of some new beehives, while Matt prepares for lambing season and selects a ram with help from mum Janice. Billy the vet returns to check on the puppies before they leave for their forever homes, and the family reflects on the positive changes they have made since Janice's accident

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Kate Quilton heads to California to investigate alternative dairy products at one of California's leading producers of almond milk, before meeting a scientist who has been raising genetically engineered goats whose milk he hopes might one day save half a million lives every year. Jimmy Doherty teams up with a fish detective on a covert mission buying and testing fish in Edinburgh to make sure it is the real deal, while Matt Tebbutt finds out what goes into spreadable butter

programme.title: Kirstie's Vintage Gems
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp demonstrates how forgotten furniture from a landfill site can be transformed, as she teams up with furniture-maker Simon Bell to revive a dining-room chair using clever painting tricks

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Carol and Stephen Howell from Bristol have been going to Barbados on holiday for years and now, with retirement fast approaching, hav decided it's time to buy a holiday home in their dream location. With a budget of £275,000 Jasmine Harman helps them to find their perfect property, whether it be a stunning hillside development or beachfront apartment

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole gives Simon O'Brien a motorcycling masterclass in Wiltshire as the duo seek more items to fix up and sell on to make profits for their owners

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien visit the Cotswolds and Somerset, where finds include a set of milk churns and an old munitions shell

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: The probate detective team searches for an heir to the estate of a woman who led a glamorous life of international travel and adventure. There is the story of an East End couple who chose to raise their disabled daughter at a time when it was against conventional wisdom, plus details of unclaimed estates still awaiting heirs

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: Charlie Luxton heads to Wymeswold, Leicestershire, to meet Ally and James, a fearless couple who have taken a gamble buying a tricky site that has eaten up almost all of their cash before work has even begun. As the months turn to years, can they hold their nerve to build the country cottage of their dreams?

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition begins at Trecarne House in Liskeard, Cornwall, where owners Trish and Tom Brock-Morgan hope to demonstrate that their B&B offers the best value for money. The pair win the group's admiration by sharing their life story, but one guest stirs up intrigue by holding his cards close to his chest

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The owners spend their second night of the competition at the Magna Carta at Windsor in Berkshire, a five-star floating bed and breakfast run by Captain Dominic Read. The guests are pleasantly surprised upon arrival, but not all are impressed by the standard of the accommodation - and a cracked teapot at the breakfast table leads to trouble for the proprietor

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third B&B of the week is Ash Cottage near Leeds Castle in Kent, where the unusual decor is an eye-opener for the guests but is not everyone's cup of tea. Trish and Tom get heated over the instant coffee before owners Rayne and Chris Deal take their fellow contestants to Chatham Dockyard to try their hands at rope-making

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final guest house of the week is A Room with a View in Brighton, where owner Stephen Bull and housekeeper Joanna Harris hope their nine-room establishment will impress the contestants. Things get off to a good start when Rayne and Chris Deal are impressed by the cleanliness of the property, but things take a turn for the worse when breakfast gets the thumbs down

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners come together to confront their critics face-to-face. Hosts of Trecarne House in Cornwall Trish and Tom Brock-Morgan raise issues with Magna Carta's Dominic Read on his mark for their hosting, while he has a bone to pick with them over criticism of his five-star floating B&B. Stephen and Joanna of A Room with a View in Brighton receive complaints about their breakfast, and tensions run high as the payments are revealed and the winner is announced

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Max McMurdo restores a pair of designer chairs, while Henry Cole breathes new life into a decrepit garden table, transforming it into a colourful and valuable new one

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Elderly one-eyed South African porcupine Roxy is introduced to a new, younger male called Henning, in the hope that she will find companionship in her old age. Kifaru, the oldest eastern black rhino at Chester Zoo, meets younger female Malindi, with the expectation that they will produce offspring to increase the dwindling population, while the Caribbean flamingos are breeding again

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Tim and Margreeth visit Dick and Angel, who come up with some radical suggestions to change their business plans. Stephanie hopes to prepare for her first singles weekend by planting a romantic rose garden - if she can work the digger. Near the Pyrenees, Sophie and James need to raise funds for their ancient chateau fast, so they decide to put on a day of historic talks, dinner and a tour. Later, Dick and Angel struggle to rescue the trampoline that's been blown into their moat

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud follows the progress of a dual-purpose family and wheelchair-friendly home built by design engineer and former Paralympic swimmer Mark Butler. Mark was paralysed from the waist down following a motorcycle accident and the home he currently lives in with his family makes it virtually impossible for him to complete even the simplest of tasks around the house. He and his wife Penny want to build a cutting-edge family home, with a drive-in basement, passenger lift and accessible kitchen and bathroom. However, building such a unique property proves a huge challenge, and the strain on Mark and Penny becomes almost unbearable

programme.title: The Good Fight
programme.desc: Reddick, Boseman & Lockhart represents an Olympic hopeful edged out of the US women's swim team due to a last-minute change to the eligibility window. Adrian is approached by the DNC with a compelling offer, while Diane and Julius unite to track down Memo 618. Legal drama, starring Christine Baranski, Cush Jumbo and Delroy Lindo

programme.title: The Tudors
programme.desc: New chancellor Sir Thomas More starts cracking down on religious extremists, but Anne Boleyn convinces the King he should at least consider Martin Luther's writings. Meanwhile, Cardinal Wolsey and Queen Catherine form an uneasy alliance to try to protect their positions, but it is rumours of a conspiracy involving them, the Pope and Charles V that finally force Henry's hand

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A 71-year-old woman is rushed in after being knocked over by her husband's car, and her blood pressure has dropped critically low. Four-year-old Maddi is brought in with an injured neck after falling over on a train, and doctors worry that metal plates she already had in her neck may have shifted, causing damage to her spine. Plus, 68-year-old Bonnie arrives after hitting her head on her bedside table

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy panel show's version of the popular words-and-numbers quiz, as Sean Lock and Claudia Winkleman take on Jon Richardson and Henning Wehn. Lexicographer Susie Dent is joined by Nick Helm to adjudicate in Dictionary Corner, while Rachel Riley looks after the numbers and letters

programme.title: New Amsterdam
programme.desc: Max, Bloom and Reynolds recount a patient's past encounter that could throw them into a massive lawsuit. Iggy confronts a local middle school about its teaching policies, while Kapoor's superstitions get the better of him. Medical drama, starring Ryan Eggold

programme.title: The Good Fight
programme.desc: Reddick, Boseman & Lockhart represents an Olympic hopeful edged out of the US women's swim team due to a last-minute change to the eligibility window. Adrian is approached by the DNC with a compelling offer, while Diane and Julius unite to track down Memo 618. Legal drama, starring Christine Baranski, Cush Jumbo and Delroy Lindo

programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Detroit Steel
programme.desc: Adam buys himself a little more time on the 1979 Ford Bronco, but pleasing a Bronco expert is not going to be easy, and Pam has a big surprise for Adam

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: Mike Wolfe and Frank Fritz try to acquire Star Wars goodies in California

programme.title: Storage Hunters
programme.desc: Jesse lands some skydiving equipment, while Papa Bear struggles to fit into his latest purchase - a vintage sports car

programme.title: Storage Hunters
programme.desc: Brandon and Lori team up with Mone as they try to win a helicopter at an auction

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: The guys discover a rare 1930s coupe at Buckey's one-man ranch and the numbers are not in Mike's favour when Frank shows off his beautiful mind

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: An Evel Knievel super-fan reveals some of the legendary daredevil's most personal items

programme.title: Red Bull Soapbox Race
programme.desc: Intrepid gravity-fuelled Soapbox Racers take on Observatory Hill overlooking Helsinki Harbour. How will they handle the brutal berm, the car wash and the ferocious kickers?

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The presenters search for the world's best four-seat supercar, pitting the Aston Martin Rapide against the Porsche Panamera Turbo and Maserati Quattroporte in a series of unusual challenges in the south of England. Harry Potter star Rupert Grint and ex-Formula One driver Rubens Barrichello each do a lap in the Reasonably Priced Car and Richard Hammond puts the Chevrolet Camaro SS and Mercedes E63 AMG through their paces on the test track

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May build their own versions of motorhomes and set off for a bank holiday weekend in the West Country to try out their creations in the real world. The Audi R8 V10 Spyder also takes on the Porsche 911 Turbo Cabriolet on the test track, and American actor Andy Garcia does a lap in the Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef visits Palermo in northern Sicily, a city echoing Arab, North African, French, Spanish and Italian influences, making its cuisine unique and irresistible. Pine nuts, raisins, almonds and golden breadcrumbs are combined with flavours of sun-ripened tomatoes, lemons and the finest olive oil to create a range of unforgettable dishes. Rick witnesses locals celebrating a festival in honour of Palermo's patron saint Rosalia, and receives a cookery lesson from a real-life duchess. At home he cooks Marsala chicken, a Sicilian classic served in UK bistros since the 1960s

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Richard Hammond tests Volkswagen's Touareg off-roader, before driving a racing version of the car against a group of snowmobile riders in Sweden. James May tries to drive faster than he has ever done before in the Bugatti Veyron Super Sport, Jeremy Clarkson examines the legacy of Formula One driver Ayrton Senna, and Tom Cruise and Cameron Diaz are the Stars in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The presenters hit the road in a Jensen-Healey, a Lotus and a TVR as they chart the decline of the British sports car industry, on a journey from Norfolk to Blackpool. On the test track, Jeremy Clarkson enthuses about the Ferrari 458 Italia, and actor Jeff Goldblum is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: The comedians are challenged to taste-test some mystery baby food, Russell Howard presents a nightmarish fairy tale and Liza Tarbuck introduces a plastic lizard to a cordless drill

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Rob Brydon hosts the comedy panel show, in which team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack are joined by guests Dara O Briain, Denise Van Outen, Rhod Gilbert and Vernon Kay, who try to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves

programme.title: Room 101
programme.desc: Frank Skinner presents the comedy panel show, which sees chef and MasterChef judge John Torode, comedian Russell Howard and actress Katherine Parkinson compete to have their pet hates banished for ever. programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson subjects the Skoda Yeti to a thorough examination, while Richard Hammond charts the evolution of the Porsche 911 by pitting a modern Turbo S cabriolet against its oldest relative the Volkswagen Beetle - and the power of gravity. programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo once again ventures onto the European rail network to retrace journeys featured in George Bradshaw's 1913 publication Continental Guide. He begins in Spain, where he visits the scene of an assassination attempt at a royal wedding in Madrid, before dancing with an unusual partner in Cordoba. He investigates the story behind Bizet's Carmen at a tobacco factory in Seville, learns about Winston Churchill's tense diplomatic mission to Algeciras and finishes with tales of British espionage in Gibraltar

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow

programme.title: Chris Tarrant: Extreme Railway Journeys

Along the way he enjoys a reindeer stew with a local family and takes the controls of one of the massive locomotives that power the trains, before pressing on along railways built under the brutal regime of Joseph Stalin

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: American army wife Adrena Austin hosts the third dinner party in Colchester. She hopes her simple menu, which revisits her Armenian heritage, will be a welcome change for her guests, but while reactions to the main course are mixed, her entertainment proves a resounding success

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: James Coe throws the fourth party in Colchester, and his choice of menu proves popular from the starter onwards. programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Vegetarian Jodie Edzekiel hosts the final dinner party in Colchester, and tries to please her guests by serving them meat, provided she can bring herself to touch it. programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Rosaria bought Bella Luna to realise her son Gianfranco's ambition to become a chef, but the Italian restaurant's future looks bleak due to his lack of experience in the kitchen combined with her refusal to acknowledge the eatery's many flaws. Gordon Ramsay heads to Easton, Pennsylvania, to help the troubled pair and save the business from financial disaster

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Scarlette Douglas offers advice to Karen and Paul from Dundee, who are looking for a holiday home on Spain's stunning Mijas Costa where they can enjoy family getaways with their teenage son Nicholas. They would love to find an apartment with lots of outside space

programme.desc: Pub landlady Alison seeks a home on the Costa Dorada in Spain. Keeley feels that their current family home is no longer suitable for their family of five and longs for a larger property, but Terence loves the location of their house and thinks that renovating it would be a better option

programme.desc: Fred Sirieix sets two chefs to the near-impossible challenge of replicating a breakfast favourite - a bowl of Shreddies. Daniel Clifford and Claude Bosi both hold two Michelin stars. Both have also previously tried and failed to win a Snackmasters trophy, so this extremely difficult challenge is their chance to earn redemption. They have three weeks to work out the factory processes that give Shreddies their famous lattice pattern and layered structure, before a cook-off where the factory judges will decide the winner

programme.title: The Great Celebrity Bake Off for Stand Up to Cancer

They are then asked to create their 'happy places' out of meringue for the showstopper challenge. Who will flap at the first hurdle and who will battle their way through to be crowned star baker? Presented by Sandi Toksvig and Noel Fielding

programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer help two couples searching the south east. English-born Tom and his Floridian wife Kaitlyn have only seen a handful of properties, so balancing his desire for a vegetable garden with her American dream of a big open-plan living space proves a difficult balance for Kirstie. Phil helps Pete and Georgie, who moved into a studio flat right before the lockdown in spring 2020 and are desperate for more space. Wanting to start a family, they're after something to grow into, but Pete is conscious of value for money

Rhinoceros hornbill Liv and partner Manu are yet to produce any offspring, but when they are moved to a new nest, Liv quickly produces a fresh clutch of egg. Meanwhile, clumsy seahorse Seabiscuit only has a week to attract a female, so he has time to incubate the eggs and give birth in time for the next full moon

programme.desc: Sue Smith hosts the fourth dinner party from Bury St Edmunds, Suffolk, and decides to organise an evening of entertainment revolving around a 'back to school' theme. However, the items on her menu puzzle the guests

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay revisits three restaurants he helped that were among the biggest challenges he faced, finding out whether they stayed on the road to recovery or turned back into nightmares. First he meets sisters Rita and Lisa to see if they have continued serving fresh instead of frozen food at Boston's La Galleria 33, before heading to the Olde Hitching Post in Hanson, Massachusetts, to discover whether controlling owner Tom has kept his promise and handed over the reins to his daughter Andrea. Finally, Gordon goes back to the Prohibition gastro pub in Everett, Washington, to catch up with owner Rishi and learn if she has ditched belly-dancing in favour of running her business

programme.title: A Place in the Sun

Jean Johansson lines up five potential properties for their budget of £200,000, but will any of them seem the perfect place?

programme.desc: Bev Cameron has multiple sclerosis and finds that a warm climate helps her manage her symptoms. As a response, she has decided to buy a property near the historic harbour town of Paphos, in Cyprus. Enlisting the help of Bev's best friend Shelley, Laura Hamilton delivers five potential homes for her budget of £90,000, in the hopes that one of them will fit the bill

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Revisiting some of the most compelling and memorable stories from the past five years, beginning with a series of extraordinary births caught on camera at Chester Zoo. Rhinoceros hornbill Liv and partner Manu are yet to produce any offspring, but when they are moved to a new nest, Liv quickly produces a fresh clutch of egg. Meanwhile, clumsy seahorse Seabiscuit only has a week to attract a female, so he has time to incubate the eggs and give birth in time for the next full moon

programme.title: First Man (2018)
programme.desc: A look at the life of astronaut Neil Armstrong, from his early days in 1961 as a NASA test pilot. By the late 1960s, as he becomes emotionally distant from his family, he embarks on a space mission that leads to him becoming the first man to walk on the moon. Director Damien Chazelle's Oscar-winning drama based on the book by James R Hansen, starring Ryan Gosling and Claire Foy

programme.desc: Architecture student Priti has trouble finding a man in the academic world and wants someone older, rugged and tattooed. No stranger to the tattooist's chair is Australian entrepreneur Nick - but will the age gap end up being a problem for these two singletons? Firefighter Katie and her date - also called Katie - open up about significant turning points in their lives, while superhero fanatic Richard thinks he has hit the jackpot when his date turns out to be a self-confessed geek and Comic Con fan

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: Anna Richardson presents the dating show with a difference, in which another two singletons take their pick of potential partners based purely on their naked bodies. programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: Anna Richardson returns with the daring dating show - and this time it promises to be even more revealing as two singletons choose a date from six naked people whose bare bodies are revealed from the feet up. programme.title: A Place in the Sun

They want somewhere that's close to both the beach and all the action for their budget of £150,000. Jasmine Harman lines up five potential properties for them to choose between

programme.title: The Great Celebrity Bake Off for Stand Up to Cancer
programme.desc: Comedian James Acaster, actor Russell Tovey, TV presenter Rylan Clark-Neal and actress Michelle Keegan face three baking tasks set by judges Prue Leith and Paul Hollywood, starting with topped flapjacks for the signature challenge and a tricky technical. They are then asked to create their 'happy places' out of meringue for the showstopper challenge. Who will flap at the first hurdle and who will battle their way through to be crowned star baker? Presented by Sandi Toksvig and Noel Fielding

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer help two couples searching in the Scottish capital. Zoe and Jason are returning to their native Edinburgh after a decade living and working in Hong Kong. They have £950,000 but that will only buy them a flat around their preferred area. Can Phil fulfil their expensive wishlist? Kirstie is with Tan, from Lisbon, and his Fife-born wife Stephanie, who cannot agree on a countryside feel or city vibe

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on what they have been watching during the week, as cameras capture their instant reactions prompted by the week's biggest and best shows, be they entertainment juggernauts, hard-hitting documentaries, news reports or gritty dramas

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Katherine Ryan and Joe Lycett take on Johnny Vegas and Sean Lock in the words-and-numbers game, while would-be novelist Christopher Bliss joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. Hosted by Jimmy Carr with Rachel Riley looking after the numbers and letters

programme.title: First Dates Hotel
programme.desc: Architecture student Priti has trouble finding a man in the academic world and wants someone older, rugged and tattooed. No stranger to the tattooist's chair is Australian entrepreneur Nick - but will the age gap end up being a problem for these two singletons? Firefighter Katie and her date - also called Katie - open up about significant turning points in their lives, while superhero fanatic Richard thinks he has hit the jackpot when his date turns out to be a self-confessed geek and Comic Con fan

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: Charlotte Ritchie, Jamali Maddix, Lee Mack, Mike Wozniak and Sarah Kendall continue their quest to curry favour with omnipotent Taskmaster Greg Davies. Jamali decorates the caravan, Sarah is appalled by a kiwi fruit and, under severe pressure, Mike delivers a revelation so visceral that it leaves everyone in a state of horrified delirium. As usual, Alex Horne offers the intrepid contenders moral support

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Fifty-four-year-old James is rushed to A&E after a head on collision with his car windscreen, leaving doctors concerned he may have sustained a serious life changing injury. Elsewhere, Justice waits with his girlfriend Elissa to have a worrying lump on his finger examined

programme.title: The Dog House
programme.desc: Lonesome teenager Jasmine seeks a canine companion to replace her twin sister Shola, who's leaving her to start a university course. Her family thinks that pedigree beagle Zoe will make the perfect new sibling, but Jasmine isn't so sure. Elsewhere, staff of Wood Green animal charity believe Jim the poodle could prove the perfect match for 12-year-old Lexi, who is looking for a 'bedtime buddy'

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Entertainment News on 5
programme.desc: Showbiz news and gossip

programme.title: Rich Kids Go Skint
programme.desc: A wealthy man with an expensive taste for designer clothes moves in with a Swansea single father on benefits, and has to get to grips with his dislike of animals

programme.title: Filthy Britain SOS
programme.desc: Anna and John have to work hard to get to the root of an almighty blockage threatening to send a tsunami of waste gushing into a bungalow. In Lewes, East Sussex, after one of the biggest bonfire night celebrations in the country, filth fighters reveal the trade secrets of clearing up the mess left by the 80,000 revellers

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: Susan and Gary bought an East End flat to let out and were approached by a letting agent who promised hassle-free guaranteed rent. But before they knew it, he had broken in and changed the locks. Now they are fighting to get the property back. Meanwhile, landlord Paul discovers that his seriously ill father's long-standing tenant owes a staggering £20,000 in rent for living in her Notting Hill home

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: Kent couple Rachel and Chris are pushed to the limit when their relationship with their tenant breaks down and she stops paying rent, leaving them more than £5,000 out of pocket. Now they need to get her out or they could lose the house. Elsewhere, a tenant uses scare tactics to keep his landlord off his back, and in Harrow, council housing officers investigate a residential property that has been turned into a brothel

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Brian O'Shaughnessy and Graham Aldred's first case sees them in a Brighton health spa as they search for assets to seize in pursuit of a £20,000 debt. Following a stand-off, they devise a way to work with the owner and her professional advisers to come up with a solution. Later, Graham reveals his softer side when he revisits a woman who defaulted on a debt payment plan

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: The high-speed police interception unit pursues a stolen car through the suburbs of Essex until the thieves turn into a cul-de-sac and, though seemingly trapped, still try to escape. Elsewhere, officers deal with a fight outside a nightclub and tackle boy racers in Southend

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Specially trained officers try to stop a 4x4 as it is driven dangerously through the streets of Basildon, and pursue a fake ambulance. In the air above Essex, the police helicopter trails a pair of teenage car thieves

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Two new officers chase a gang of youths spotted joyriding around an Essex village in a stolen car. A counsellor is called out to avert a potential suicide on the M25, and a thief's attempts to misdirect the team fall flat when damning evidence of his crimes is found in his car

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: High Court enforcement officers Paul and Steve's first job is an eviction with a twist when they find out the landlord is the tenant's son, while their next case is also tricky and involves family members with a question mark over their immigration status who are now facing homelessness. Their final case involves a man with a history of mental illness who has reached the end of his tenancy but refuses to leave, and Steve tries to find a solution after a church minister and his mother fail to placate him

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Documentary series giving an insight into the world of debt collection. Kevin Stokes and Brian O'Shaughnessy try to collect a debt from a technology company, but with the director nowhere to be seen, the office manager does everything she can to prove the business is no longer trading. Stewart McCracken and Elmor Victor face a heartbreaking house repossession in Merseyside, where two landlords are forced to evict a tenant recovering from a brain tumour which has cost him his job and income

programme.title: Neighbours
programme.desc: With Karl charged by the police, Bea and Susan start to realise Olivia's book might be the only way to clear his name. Aaron controversially announces he wants to name the baby Fay

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Lewis is about to end Ari's life, but is interrupted by Mia. Mac arrives home and starts researching unexpected babies. Justin and Leah anticipate the house handover

programme.title: Britain's Parking Hell
programme.desc: In the final episode, the Weston-super-Mare parking team deal with a crime spree which has seen blue badge fraud increase significantly in the last year alone. Meanwhile, mother and daughter Lowri and Bethan in Cardiff struggle to find a parking space on the day of a big rugby match, and residents of Basildon are angered about a private parking company that has issued over a thousand fines in a single month

programme.title: Criminals: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Journalist Nick Wallis joins police forces across the UK to discover how CCTV and technological advances are helping to fight the huge rise in street crime. In London, the Met's robbery squads work the streets on a busy Friday night, and with criminals now using motorbikes to get away, cameras help officers identify, track and apprehend offenders. Nick also finds out how pickpockets, scammers and bag thieves use sleight of hand and distraction to relieve people of their valuables

programme.title: A&E After Dark
programme.desc: Medical staff are faced with an aggressive patient who was brought in by police after being pushed down a flight of concrete stairs, and is shouting and swearing. Doctors are concerned that his behaviour could be as a result of a serious head injury. Doctor Andrew administers morphine to help a woman in agony, but the medics grow worried when her screams turn to silence

programme.title: 999: Critical Condition
programme.desc: Return of the documentary following the work of medical staff at Stoke's Royal University Hospital, as they strive to save the lives of sick and injured patients. A one-punch attack in the street leaves a man with a potentially devastating head injury, and trauma team leader Richard quickly realises he needs to perform emergency surgery on a bleed inside his brain. A young man arrives with multiple broken bones and suspected internal injuries following a motorbike accident, while a factory worker hopes the team can save his arm after a horrific accident with a circular saw

programme.title: Rain Man (1988)
programme.desc: Brash yuppie car dealer Charlie discovers his deceased father has left the family fortune to Raymond, the autistic elder brother he never knew he had. In a bid to get his hands on the money, he tracks down and abducts Raymond - but the ensuing cross-country journey proves to be a profound learning experience. Oscar-winning drama, starring Tom Cruise and Dustin Hoffman, with Valeria Golino, Gerald R Molen and Jack Murdock

programme.title: Filthy Britain SOS
programme.desc: Midlands-based pest exterminators Chris and Mark take on some elusive mice causing havoc in an unexpected location, while in Buckinghamshire, a lorry load of potentially hazardous waste is dumped by the side of a main road, and boss David suspects it could be linked to organised crime

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: The tale of an elderly couple whose hopes of a peaceful retirement were ruined when their tenants stopped paying rent, then moved in their abusive friends who became squatters. With the house becoming a free-for-all and in a terrible state of repair, and the couple facing a barrage of verbal threats and abusive behaviour, they have been forced to seek help in an attempt to get their property back. Plus, a man who cannot sell his house because his sitting tenant refuses to leave

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: In Bristol, eviction lawyer Chris deals with a complex case in which squatters have broken into an empty pub and are now refusing to leave, with landlord Alex wanting them out as he is awaiting planning permission to turn the pub into flats. Jill is trying to regain a property she and her late husband bought as an investment, which she has been renting out to alleviate financial stress

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Documentary series giving an insight into the world of debt collection. Stewart McCracken and Elmor Victor face a heartbreaking house repossession in Merseyside, where two landlords are forced to evict a tenant recovering from a brain tumour which has cost him his job and income

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: A stolen car is recovered on the M11, and the team is given advanced training in the use of a tactical battering ram. Plus, a suspect briefly goes missing at a burger bar, an officer proves his mettle in a series of drug raids, and members of the unit are forced to calm a drunken sailor

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: The team is forced to intervene when a man is assaulted in the street by his wife. Officers set out on the trail of one of the largest stolen car operations in Essex, and the interceptors put their vehicles to the test when the driver of a hijacked 4x4 goes off road in an attempt to lose them

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Kevin Stokes and Brian O'Shaughnessy get into a doorstep confrontation with a debtor who wants to prove her family business is bankrupt. However, when tempers flare Brian is assaulted and the agents call the police. Elsewhere, Paul Bohill and Steve Pinner face evicting people from three properties owned by the same landlord in north London, and Brian and Delroy Anglin collect £2,000 debt owed to a vet in Kent

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!

In Essex, a couple owe their neighbour £11,000, and on the south coast, debt collection from a Turkish restaurant gets complicated when there is confusion about ownership

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Mac is not sure if he should be telling Ari about the pregnancy yet. Amber and Dean are finally getting along, and start to feel closer

programme.title: Inside Iceland: Britain's Budget Supermarket
programme.desc: On the search for a high-end restaurant-style chip to add to their existing range, Iceland's chip buyer heads to Belgium to see how to produce a premium chip cheaply. There's also a look at the company's out-of-town bulk-buying spin-off called Food Warehouse, and cameras reveal how the supermarket chain is keen to cash in on the popularity of sea bass

programme.title: Bargain Brits on Benefits
programme.desc: Juanita scours for designer goods at bargain prices and Lee swaps a house and bills for living rent-free in a horse box

programme.title: Filthy House SOS
programme.desc: Francis and Brennan help a compulsive buyer who has been housebound for 10 years owing to health complications, and whose Newport home is now impossible to clean

programme.title: 22 Kids & Counting
programme.desc: With 10 teenagers now living in the Radford house, their laziness is driving mum Sue to take up DIY projects, leaving the house in chaos as they drag on. Sue wants a third French bulldog and while Noel says that isn't going to happen, she gets her own way, and there is another addition to the family, albeit just a new puppy

programme.title: Gypsy Kids: Our Secret World
programme.desc: Childhood in gypsy and traveller communities. Eleven-year-old Albert prepares for his first competitive boxing match, keen to impress his father and earn himself a solid reputation in the community. Twins Johnny and Abraham take part in a catapult shooting contest, while Sheri-Anne is trying to master the art of standing on the back of a moving horse

programme.title: Selling Sex to Survive
programme.desc: Documentary following the lives of people who have been driven to prostitution by poverty, as well as the organisations dedicated to helping them to turn their lives around, including volunteers like former sex worker Sonia, who now uses her past experiences to help others in similar circumstances

programme.title: Bargain Loving Brits by the Sea
programme.desc: The real stories of more people living the high life by the sea on a low budget. B&B owner Chris has dropped his prices to keep turnover high. Will an incoming hen party be happy with his cheap-and-cheerful approach? Ian tries to attract punters to his donkey ride business, Sharon's dream of running the town's first Caribbean restaurant proves to be a tall order, and at her static home on a retirement park, Sandra organises a posthumous birthday party for her late husband

programme.title: Casualty 24/7

programme.desc: Eight more privileged youngsters spend a week on the breadline, including Maria - daughter of Russian multi-millionaires - who meets Becky and Mohammed in Croydon

programme.title: Filthy Britain SOS
programme.desc: Newbie Chloe is taken under the wing of experienced enforcers Ian and Anneil as they conduct a street sweep, deep cleaning Hounslow's problem areas

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: Harrow Council carries out early morning raids on several dangerous properties, only to find vulnerable tenants in uninhabitable conditions, including one man living in a plywood shed on a roof. Plus, two more cases of nightmare tenants. Nisha lost her job just as her tenants stopped paying the rent, while Andy agreed to rent his north London home to a friend - who ended up subletting the house and pocketing the profits for himself

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: A young mum locked out of her home by the young couple she allowed to stay there for free while the wife had a baby. Plus, an east London landlord who found half a dozen strangers living in his house, after his original tenant had run off and sublet the property. Meanwhile, Harrow Council raid a small two-bedroom flat in unsafe conditions

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Agents Paul Bohill and Steve Pinner get caught up in a dispute between a landlord and a tenant who refuses to move out, and Brian O'Shaughnessy and Delroy Anglin chase a £17,000 debt. In Essex, a couple owe their neighbour £11,000, and on the south coast, debt collection from a Turkish restaurant gets complicated when there is confusion about ownership

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Inspector Emma Brookes races 40 miles to prevent a suicidal man throwing himself off a rooftop, while one of her colleagues chases a suspected drug dealer. Elsewhere, the Air Support Unit is called to track a fugitive car thief, and a man is caught with a van full of stolen clothes

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Two officers make a surprising discovery while apprehending a gang of car thieves, and a team uses automatic number-plate recognition to identify stolen vehicles outside the biggest shopping centre in Essex. An air ambulance makes an emergency landing in Clacton-on-Sea, a three-car shunt brings the M25 to a halt and a policeman drops his handcuffs down a drain

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Officers conduct a 48-hour hunt for a thief who stole a 4x4, and their perseverance pays off when they manage to catch the culprit. Meanwhile, in Clacton-on-Sea, a tip-off sends the interceptors to a drug den, and the Essex chopper team co-ordinates a manhunt when three high-profile suspects evade capture by fleeing into a field full of cows

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: In east London, High Court Enforcement Agents Paul and Phil are involved in a brothel eviction, while in south Wales, Stewart and Elmore chase a debt owed by a working mum. Another case sees Stewart and Victor hunting down a £3,000 debt owed by a Liverpool takeaway for rent arrears

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Paul Bohill and Phil Short must deal with a negligent tenant who fled upon their arrival, forcing the agents to set a trap in order to evict him. Brian O'Shaughnessy and Kevin Stokes chase a debt of £5,000 in Kent, before heading to Surrey to recover £11,000 owed by the owner a bespoke furniture business. Plus, Paul and Steve Pinner's patience is tested to the limit when they are sent to carry out an eviction in London

programme.title: Neighbours
programme.desc: Afraid of her feelings, Nicolette shuts down Chloe and tries to avoid opening up to her. Roxy runs into Holden at the Tram, and then discovers the gang's encampment

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Ryder and Chloe confront each other over their kiss. Dean and Amber enjoy a successful night together, and give romance another go

programme.title: Bargain Loving Brits by the Sea
programme.desc: The stories of more budget lifestyles in the bright lights of Blackpool. Chambermaid-turned-B&B owner Claire faces a big challenge when a group of VIP business leaders book out her entire hotel. Glaswegian Sammy has lived in Blackpool for 33 years, and now he runs the only caravan site in the town centre, while Blondie tribute artist Fiona moved to the resort with dreams of making it big in the music industry, but has to travel miles out of town to get gigs

programme.title: Casualty 24/7
programme.desc: Doctors treat an elderly patient rushed into hospital suffering from a racing heartbeat, and a 28-year-old man receives treatment after a motorbike accident. Nurse Richard Jackson meets Graham, a patient with a complex medical history, who has been brought in with the symptoms of a stroke

programme.title: 999: Critical Condition
programme.desc: Trauma team leader Julie Norton battles to save two patients whose lives hang in the balance. Father of four Ian is rushed in after suffering a cardiac arrest, while another man is bleeding so heavily he risks losing his entire circulatory system. Meanwhile, Ken has fallen from a roof onto a skip, leaving him with a gaping wound to his thigh and potentially life-threatening injuries to his pelvis and spine, and Dr Nayak helps Michael, who has suffered a severe stroke

programme.title: A&E After Dark
programme.desc: A young man has to be restrained by police officers and security personnel in the ambulance bay after shouting and swearing at medical staff. Simon is rushed to hospital with serious injuries after being thrown off his horse and cart at high speed, and an initial CT scan reveals the 73-year-old has multiple broken bones

programme.title: Ambulance: Code Red
programme.desc: A driver is trapped in his car after it careered out of control, smashing into a wall before flipping sideways onto a driveway. Dr Polly Haywood and critical care paramedic Sarah Folley are sent to the scene

programme.title: Celebrity Botched Up Bodies
programme.desc: The case of Shameless star Tina Malone (Mimi), who opted for a gastric band and lost more than 11 stone, only to be left with a vast spread of excess skin and a need for corrective surgery. Plus, singer Pete Burns talks about his addiction to plastic surgery and, in an archive interview, Dolly Parton discusses the reasons for her changing image

programme.title: Feeders
programme.desc: Part one of two. Cameras focus on people paid to gain weight by a group of devoted admirers, including 22-stone Goddexx Althea from the North East of England

programme.title: The Disappearance
programme.desc: Luke and Helen continue looking for suspects connected to Henry's former work, and Catherine finds comfort in a terminal patient

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: Jessica is sued for the wrongful conviction and subsequent death of a prison escapee, and acts quickly to get herself off the hook. Angela Lansbury stars as the amateur sleuth, with Kim Hunter and Carrie Hamilton

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Bishop is more determined than ever to seek revenge for the murder of Qasim, her deceased boyfriend, after Gibbs and the team find a new lead in the Chen case. Meanwhile, Torres teaches the agents the art of pickpocketing

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: McGee discovers his flat has been torn apart by a robber trying to locate an item of great value hidden somewhere by the convicted criminal who previously owned the unit. Meanwhile, congress woman Flemming tries to persuade Director Vance to pursue a career in politics. Mary Stuart Masterson guest stars

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: When a wealthy art patron is found murdered and her body mutilated, the team's inquiries lead to failed painter Larry Brunig, who objected to the obscene nature of the art supported and promoted by the victim - and a probe into his past reveals a violent history

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Briscoe and Green investigate the discovery of a prostitute's body behind a rubbish bin. As inquiries get under way, they uncover a seedy case involving the shipping of illegal immigrants, police corruption and virtual sex

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A baby is found dead in his cot, and reports indicate he was poisoned. All the evidence pins the blame on a British au pair employed by the parents, but she claims the infant's death was a result of the mother's negligence. Sam Waterston stars

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Van Buren orders Briscoe and Green to reinvestigate unsolved cases, starting with the murder of a teenager 20 years previously. However, they discover much of the evidence gathered at the time has vanished, and the detective in charge of the case may have pursued an innocent man to protect the real culprit. An unexpected confession allows the case to end up in court again, but legal trickery makes a conviction an unlikely prospect

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A man with a history of violence is arrested for the murder of his son's school ice-hockey coach, but pleads mitigating circumstances. Helped by a glib defence lawyer, he claims that he committed the crime while suffering from a previously unknown mental condition he calls 'sports rage'

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Briscoe and Curtis investigate the murder of a rare coin dealer, focusing their efforts on a millionaire who seems the most likely culprit - but Ross finds himself forced to play detective too. Sam Waterson and Jerry Orbach star

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: In a bid to carry out a sailor's dying wish, Gibbs and the NCIS team re-investigate a murder case that was originally ruled an accidental death. As the investigation proceeds, the case reminds Torres of a tragic time from his past

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: When a US Marine is murdered at an event for the Honor Flight Network, a non-profit that arranges for veterans to visit the Second World War, Korea and Vietnam War Memorials for free, the NCIS team members have to rely on irritable Vietnam War veteran Henry Rogers, who seems to be the key to finding the culprit

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: After a vice admiral's laptop is infected with ransomware, he enlists McGee and the NCIS team to track down the hacker before the computer virus eliminates his personal and professional files. Bruce Boxleitner guest stars

programme.title: Law & Order: Criminal Intent
programme.desc: Goren and Eames are assigned to investigate the murder of a woman who was poisoned by an anonymous package, and unearth a dark secret stretching back to her childhood. They also uncover a conspiracy linked to the case that could cause huge problems for an expensive campaign being organised by the Church. Guest starring Stephen Colbert

programme.title: Law & Order: Criminal Intent
programme.desc: The detectives investigate the murder of a maths student who operated an illicit card-counting system in a poker club - and their inquiries uncover a complex computer-assisted gambling system. As events unfold, even their own workstations are hit by the crooks responsible. Drama, starring Vincent D'Onofrio

programme.title: Law & Order: Criminal Intent
programme.desc: Goren and Eames investigate when a parole officer is killed in a hit-and-run. The detectives track down the last person to see her alive - an academic whose juvenile delinquent son becomes their prime suspect - but are also intrigued to learn the dead woman's family is travelling clan with a dislike of outsiders

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part one of two. Abby's homeland security think-tank is compromised when its leader is found to have been murdered, and a theoretical guide to how to pull off a terror attack is stolen

programme.title: Grimm
programme.desc: Nick investigates a double-murder with a connection to a travelling carnival where Wesen display their powers but pass them off to the crowd as clever magic tricks

programme.title: Grimm
programme.desc: Adalind and her baby return to Portland, with Nick's mother as her bodyguard. The detective is anxious about having to be the best man at Monroe's wedding

programme.title: Grimm
programme.desc: When the Verat discover that Adalind and her baby have gone to Captain Sean Renard for help, Nick enlists the help of Monroe and Rosalee to keep Adalind safe, as his mother makes plans to deal with the Royals. Fantasy crime drama, starring David Giuntoli

programme.title: Criminals: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Journalist Nick Wallis joins police forces around the nation to discover how CCTV and technological advances are helping to bring criminals to justice. programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: When a US Marine is murdered at an event for the Honor Flight Network, a non-profit that arranges for veterans to visit the Second World War, Korea and Vietnam War Memorials for free, the NCIS team members have to rely on irritable Vietnam War veteran Henry Rogers, who seems to be the key to finding the culprit

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: After a vice admiral's laptop is infected with ransomware, he enlists McGee and the NCIS team to track down the hacker before the computer virus eliminates his personal and professional files. Bruce Boxleitner guest stars

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Briscoe and Green investigate the strangulation of a college graduate who moonlighted as a stripper to pay off her fees, and learn that the girl's death is linked to insider trading involving a former porn star and her mentor. Jerry Orbach stars

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: The relatives of a wealthy woman found in a coma come under suspicion when the team investigates her subsequent death. It later transpires she was suffering from a degenerative disease, and the theory of a possible mercy killing crumbles when the family's affairs, both financial and sexual, come to light. David Dukes guest stars

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A baby is found dead in his cot, and reports indicate he was poisoned. All the evidence pins the blame on a British au pair employed by the parents, but she claims the infant's death was a result of the mother's negligence. Sam Waterston stars

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Briscoe and Green investigate the discovery of four bodies in a jeweller's shop. Suspicion soon falls on the husband of one of the victims, who is revealed to have changed his identity to escape a shady past. The shop's security tapes are subsequently discovered in his flat, but the defendant surprises everyone by insisting on representing himself in court - and makes a big impression on the jury

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Animal rights activists are implicated in the death of an Aids researcher killed by a bite from an infected monkey, and use the ensuing court case to drum up support for their cause. As events unfold, McCoy and Carmichael find themselves debating ethics with lawyers who argue the protection of animal life is a valid defence in a murder case

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Curtis uncovers evidence of police corruption that puts the DA's office into conflict with an ambitious judge, and Briscoe comes under investigation for stealing evidence from a police lockup. Crime drama, with Sam Waterston and Jerry Orbach

programme.title: NCIS

programme.title: NCIS

programme.title: NCIS

programme.desc: In a bid to carry out a sailor's dying wish, Gibbs and the NCIS team re-investigate a murder case that was originally ruled an accidental death. As the investigation proceeds, the case reminds Torres of a tragic time from his past

programme.desc: Worf and Dax embark on a hazardous mission behind enemy lines, and despite the difficulty of the journey, it proves to be almost like a honeymoon for the newlyweds. But danger is just around the corner when Jadzia is critically injured by a Jem'Hadar patrol, leaving her husband facing the almost impossible dilemma of choosing between love and duty. Sci-fi drama, with Michael Dorn and Terry Farrell

programme.title: Star Trek: Enterprise
programme.desc: The crew comes to the aid of a dying alien and Archer tries to discover more about him, unaware the being has a hidden agenda. Simmering tensions between the officers erupt during a routine training drill, and T'Pol learns about Trip's off-duty activities

programme.title: Hawaii Five-0
programme.desc: The lives of the team's informants are put in danger when the police computer system is breached, forcing McGarrett to enlist the help of a hacker he just imprisoned

programme.title: Ghost Whisperer
programme.desc: The spirit of a man buried in the wrong grave haunts a cemetery, prompting Melinda to try to reunite the ghost with his wife and daughter

programme.title: Ghost Whisperer
programme.desc: Jim takes up Melinda's job investigating the paranormal when he is haunted by a beautiful woman who appears to him carrying various Egyptian symbols

programme.title: Killer in My Village

programme.title: Britain's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The case of Joanne Dennehy, who in 2013 became the first woman in British history to be given a whole life sentence after murdering three men and attacking two others

programme.title: CSI: Crime Scene Investigation
programme.desc: Grissom investigates an explosion at a trailer park in which two people died. As the corpses are examined, it becomes clear one has been run over - and when the car is retrieved, two more bodies are discovered in the boot. Meanwhile, Catherine deals with the case of a woman found dead outside a strip club. Drama, starring William Petersen and Marg Helgenberger

programme.title: Banged Up Abroad
programme.desc: Investigating how university student Howard Marks became one of the world's biggest cannabis traffickers and how his vast empire eventually began to unravel

programme.desc: Archer permits Dr Phlox to put all the crew members into comas to allow them to pass through a trans-dimensional disturbance unscathed - though he may not be able to survive the ordeal himself. Sci-fi adventure, starring Scott Bakula and John Billingsley

programme.title: Underworld at War
programme.desc: Peter Williams looks at the role - and the unusual odd jobs - of country policemen in the South and South East during the Second World War, when rumours of chicken stealing and illegal pig roasts were fairly common diversions from the privations of rationing

programme.title: The Food Hospital
programme.desc: A 47-year-old man receives a shock when consultant surgeon Shaw Somers reveals he is suffering from metabolic syndrome and his body is more like that of a 60-year-old. A 23-year-old woman seeks help with her extremely bad breath, GP Giovanni Miletto puts together an experimental plan to combat chronic fatigue syndrome, and Pixie McKenna discovers how diet can influence people's chances of developing cancer

programme.desc: Two men visit a gambler - each with a reason to kill him

programme.desc: Obsessive skipper Captain Ahab leads his ship and crew on an epic quest to find and kill the legendary white whale that bit off his leg - but his lust for vengeance endangers all their lives. John Huston's seafaring adventure, based on Herman Melville's novel, starring Gregory Peck, Richard Baseheart, Orson Welles and Leo Genn

Comedy, starring Bernard Hill, Virginia McKenna, Simon Callow and Sue Johnston

programme.title: Closing the Ring (2007)
programme.desc: An old woman consumed by grief following the death of her husband receives a message from a stranger who claims to have found a ring belonging to her that was lost decades before. The discovery brings to light secrets from her past and causes her to look back to the beginnings of her marriage. Drama, starring Shirley MacLaine, Christopher Plummer and Neve Campbell

programme.title: Cruel Passion (1977)
programme.desc: Two sisters are expelled from a nunnery and are plunged into a life of debauchery. While one embraces her new lifestyle, the other is desperate to cling to her old morality and sees a hope of salvation in a mysterious aristocrat who harbours a hidden dark side. Historical drama based on the novel Justine by the Marquis de Sade, starring Koo Stark and Martin Potter

Second World War adventure, starring Jack Hawkins and John Gregson

programme.title: The Moodys
programme.desc: The family throws a surprise 40th birthday party for Bridget and it's up to Roger to keep her in the dark. programme.title: The Moodys
programme.desc: As the family takes an Easter trip to a holiday park on the coast, Kev sets up a camp so impressive it earns him an invitation to join the Australian Cruising Drivers Club, while a mistake with the booking leaves Sean and three others facing the weekend in a cramped motorhome. Comedy, starring Patrick Brammall

programme.title: The Adventures of Barry McKenzie (1972)
programme.desc: A brash Australian travels to Britain with his prim aunt to claim his inheritance, causing outrage among the Brits wherever he goes and tangling with religious charlatans, music producers and a pompous BBC executive. Comedy based on the Private Eye comic strip, starring Barry Crocker, Peter Cook and Spike Milligan. Creator Barry Humphries appears in several roles - including Edna Everage

programme.title: K-Shop (2016)
programme.desc: A Bournemouth takeaway owner despairs of his drunken, abusive customers and is finally pushed to the limit when his father is killed in a violent altercation. He begins murdering the people who make his life a misery, and disposes of the bodies by making them into kebabs. Horror, starring Reece Noi and Ewen MacIntosh

programme.title: Inside Claridge's
programme.desc: Regular guest Alexander, Crown Prince of Yugoslavia, returns to stay in the suite in which he was born in 1945. General manager Thomas Kochs takes any change very seriously and oversees every decision necessary - from new alarm clocks to a £10million renovation plan

programme.title: EFL Highlights
programme.desc: Brentford v Yeovil Town. Action from the 2012/13 League One play-off final at Wembley Stadium, where the remaining place in the following season's Championship was decided. Brentford agonisingly missed out on automatic promotion on the final day of the regular campaign, but recovered to defeat Swindon Town on penalties in their semi-final, while Yeovil were 2-1 aggregate victors over Sheffield United

programme.title: EFL Highlights
programme.desc: Bradford City v Northampton Town. Action from the 2012/13 League Two play-off final at Wembley Stadium, where promotion to League One awaited the winners. The Bantams were making their second appearance of the season at Wembley having also reached the League Cup final following an outstanding giant-killing run, and they needed to come from behind to edge out Burton Albion to reach this stage. The Cobblers ended a run of recent poor form to win 1-0 in both legs of their semi-final against Cheltenham Town to move within one match of reaching the third tier of English football

programme.title: Live Premier League
programme.desc: Chelsea v Brighton & Hove Albion (Kick-off 8.00pm). Coverage of the top-flight match at Stamford Bridge, as both teams at either ends of the table battle it out for three important points. Thomas Tuchel's previous home game saw the end to his unbeaten run as Chelsea manager, thanks to a stunning performance from West Brom. Three second-half goals from the Baggies ended a run of 14 matches without defeat for the new Blues manager.

programme.desc: Aston Villa v Manchester City (Kick-off 8.15pm). Coverage of the top-flight match at Villa Park, as Pep Guardiola's side look to take another step towards a third title in four years. Goals from Bernardo Silva and Ilkay Gundogan were enough to help City to their sixth successive league win.

programme.title: Live Premier League Darts
programme.desc: Coverage of Judgement Night in Milton Keynes, which will see the first eliminations of the 2021 campaign. Last years tournament, which didn't finish until mid-October due to the coronavirus pandemic, was won by Englishman Glen Durrant for the first time in his career. He defeated Nathan Apsinall in a thrilling final, eventually coming out 11-8 winner in the showpiece held behind closed doors in Coventry.
